description: 一种进行性神经退行性疾病，影响运动神经元。

name: 肝内胆管癌
description: 一种起源于肝内胆管上皮的恶性肿瘤，在本研究中与LINE-1的体细胞插入相关。

name: 肝硬化
description: 肝硬化是一种慢性进行性肝病。

name: 肝细胞癌
description: 肝细胞癌是一种原发性肝癌。

name: 肺癌
description: 一种起源于肺部细胞的恶性肿瘤，文中以A549细胞系作为研究对象。

name: 肺腺癌
description: 肺腺癌是肺癌的一种主要亚型，起源于肺部的腺体细胞。

name: 肿瘤
description: 肿瘤是由细胞异常增殖形成的组织团块，包括良性肿瘤和恶性肿瘤。

name: 肿瘤发生
description: 肿瘤形成的过程，与基因组不稳定性和L1再激活风险增加相关。

name: 肿瘤疾病
description: 摘要中提及的与L1逆转录转座相关的疾病类别。

name: 胃癌
description: 发生在胃部的恶性肿瘤。

name: 胃肠道癌症
description: 发生在胃、结直肠和食道等消化道的恶性肿瘤。

name: 胎盘功能障碍
description: 胎盘结构或功能异常，影响胚胎发育。

name: 胚胎性肿瘤
description: 胚胎性肿瘤是一组起源于胚胎或原始神经上皮细胞的中枢神经系统恶性肿瘤。

name: 胰腺导管腺癌
description: 一种胰腺癌，是摘要中研究的疾病模型。

name: 胰腺癌
description: 一种发生在胰腺的恶性肿瘤，与LINE-1插入活动相关的表达特征与患者较差的总生存期相关。

name: 胰腺神经内分泌肿瘤
description: 一种发生在胰腺的神经内分泌肿瘤。

name: 脑发育的体细胞突变疾病
description: 摘要中提到的已知的脑发育相关的体细胞突变疾病，其分布模式与研究中发现的克隆模式相似。

name: 脑癌
description: 起源于脑组织的癌症。

name: 膀胱癌
description: 一种发生在膀胱的恶性肿瘤。

name: 自发遗传病
description: 由有害的逆转录转座事件诱发的遗传性疾病。

name: 自身免疫性疾病
description: 一类因免疫系统错误攻击自身组织而引发的疾病。

name: 自身免疫疾病
description: 免疫系统错误攻击自身健康组织的疾病。

name: 自闭症
description: 自闭症是一种神经发育障碍，本文研究了其大脑中LINE-1表达的变化。

name: 自闭症谱系障碍
description: 一种神经发育障碍，其特征是社会交往和沟通困难以及重复性行为。

name: 血友病A
description: 一种由于凝血因子VIII基因缺陷或功能障碍导致的遗传性出血性疾病。

name: 血友病B
description: 血友病B是一种因凝血因子IX缺乏或功能异常导致的遗传性出血性疾病。

name: 血癌
description: 起源于血液或造血组织的癌症。

name: 衰老
description: 一种与年龄增长相关的生理功能衰退过程。

name: 认知任务表现受损
description: 由甲基苯丙胺滥用引起的、由纹状体介导的多种认知任务表现下降。

name: 遗传不稳定性
description: 由ERCC1或XPF基因缺陷个体表现出的基因组不稳定状态。

name: 遗传性疾病
description: 摘要中提及，不受控制的逆转录转座事件可能解释遗传性疾病的发病。

name: 遗传疾病
description: 由基因突变引起的疾病，包括由LINE-1插入导致的单基因疾病。

name: 遗传病
description: 由遗传物质改变引起的疾病，可通过生殖细胞传递给后代。

name: 非小细胞肺癌
description: 全球最常见的肺癌形式，具有侵袭性，当前诊断方法成本高、易出现假阳性，且常出现治疗耐药性。

name: 面肩肱型肌营养不良症
description: 面肩肱型肌营养不良症是一种由DUX4基因在骨骼肌中异常表达引起的遗传性肌肉疾病。

name: 额颞叶变性
description: 一组以额叶和颞叶进行性萎缩为特征的神经退行性疾病。

name: 食管癌
description: 发生在食道的恶性肿瘤。

name: 食管腺癌
description: 一种发生在食管腺体组织的恶性肿瘤。

name: 食管鳞状细胞癌
description: 摘要中研究的癌症类型，是食管癌中最常见的形式，通常诊断时已到晚期。

name: 骨髓增生异常综合征
description: 一种骨髓造血功能异常的克隆性疾病，可进展为急性髓系白血病。

name: 髓系恶性肿瘤
description: 泛指起源于髓系造血细胞的一类恶性肿瘤，包括骨髓增生异常综合征和急性髓系白血病等。

name: 髓系白血病
description: 本文研究的白血病类型，文中描述了LINE-1在其中具有肿瘤抑制功能。

name: 高级别卵巢浆液性癌
description: 一种卵巢癌的亚型，本研究中检测到体细胞LINE-1逆转录转座事件。

name: 高级别浆液性卵巢癌
description: 一种常见且侵袭性强的卵巢癌亚型。

name: 高级别胶质瘤
description: 高级别胶质瘤是一类恶性程度高、预后较差的中枢神经系统胶质瘤。

name: 3' UTR stem-loop structure
description: A conserved RNA secondary structure at the 3' untranslated region (UTR) of L1 and Alu elements, hypothesized to be recognized by the LINE-1 reverse transcriptase.

name: 3' UTR-mediated posttranscriptional gene regulation
description: Posttranscriptional control of mRNA subcellular localization, stability, and translation mediated via recognition of a given mRNA transcript's 3' untranslated region (UTR) by microRNAs and RNA-binding proteins.

name: 3' UTR依赖的逆转录转座
description: 某些LINE-1亚型（严格型）进行逆转录转座所必需的机制。

name: 3' UTR茎环结构
description: 在LINE-1和Alu转座子3'非翻译区发现的高度保守的RNA二级结构，可能被LINE-1逆转录酶识别，对逆转录转座至关重要。

name: 3' end processing
description: 3' end processing refers to the molecular mechanism that determines the termination point of LINE-1 retrotransposition, which can involve bypassing the primary polyadenylation signal.

name: 3' flanking sequence mobilization
description: The process by which retrotransposition-competent L1s can mobilize and retrotranspose non-L1 sequences located downstream (3') of their own genomic location to new genomic sites.

name: 3' poly(A) tract requirement
description: The presence of a 3' polyadenosine tract is critical for the retrotransposition of LINE-1 and its trans-mobilized targets.

name: 3' poly(A)尾依赖性
description: LINE-1转座子及其反式动员的RNA的3'端需要一个多聚腺苷酸尾，该结构对于其逆转录转座活性至关重要。

name: 3' transduction
description: A process mediated by the L1 machinery where neighboring, nonrepetitive sequences are mobilized along with the L1 element.

name: 3'-end stem-loop formation
description: 3'-end stem-loop formation refers to the folding of the 3' end of an RNA or DNA molecule into a secondary structure consisting of a base-paired stem and a single-stranded loop.

name: 3'-末端茎环结构
description: 位于RNA或DNA序列3'末端的二级结构，由互补碱基配对形成的茎部和未配对的环部组成。

name: 3'末端添加额外胞嘧啶
description: 人类LINE-1逆转录酶在合成cDNA第一链的3'末端时，会添加一个额外的胞嘧啶（C）残基。

name: 3'端多聚腺苷酸化
description: 在LINE-1元件的3'末端添加多聚腺苷酸尾的过程，是其完成逆转录转座的关键步骤。

name: 3'端转导
description: LINE-1逆转录转座过程中，将其3'端侧翼的非L1 DNA序列一同转座到新基因组位置的现象。

name: 3D chromatin architecture
description: 3D chromatin architecture refers to the three-dimensional organization of chromatin within the nucleus.

name: 3D chromatin structure formation
description: 3D chromatin structure formation refers to the spatial organization of chromatin within the nucleus, which influences gene regulation.

name: 3D genome architecture influence
description: The process by which transposons affect the three-dimensional spatial organization of the genome within the nucleus.

name: 3D genome reorganization
description: 3D genome reorganization refers to changes in the three-dimensional architecture of chromatin, including enhancer-promoter loops and topologically associating domain (TAD) boundaries.

name: 3D nuclear organization
description: 3D nuclear organization refers to the spatial arrangement of chromatin and nuclear compartments within the nucleus.

name: 5' UTR启动子活性
description: LINE-1转座子5'非翻译区具有驱动自身转录的启动子功能。

name: 5' UTR活性
description: LINE-1的5'非翻译区在基因表达调控中的作用。

name: 5' and 3' transduction
description: 5' and 3' transduction is a process by which retroelements shuffle genomic regions.

name: 5' end microhomology
description: 5' end microhomology refers to short stretches of sequence identity between the 5' end of a new L1 integrant and the target DNA sequence.

name: 5' flanking sequence transduction
description: 5' flanking sequence transduction is a process where SVA elements acquire and transduce 5' promoter sequences from external cellular promoters.

name: 5' integration
description: 5' integration refers to the molecular mechanism by which the 5' end of a LINE element is integrated into the host chromosome during retrotransposition.

name: 5' inversion
description: A genomic rearrangement where the 5' segment of a retrotransposed element is inverted relative to the 3' segment, resulting in a head-to-head orientation.

name: 5' junction formation
description: 5' junction formation refers to the mechanism for joining the retrotransposon to the target site at the 5' end during retrotransposition.

name: 5' transduction
description: 5' transduction is a process where flanking genomic sequences upstream of an L1 element are co-mobilized during retrotransposition.

name: 5' truncation
description: 5'端截短，指体细胞中LINE-1插入事件的特征，与生殖细胞多态性插入不同。

name: 5'-end attachment
description: The process of linking the 5'-end of a nascent retrotransposon copy to its genomic target site.

name: 5'-end joining
description: The molecular mechanism responsible for generating the 5' junctions of LINE inserts.

name: 5'-end-joining pathway
description: The 5'-end-joining pathway is a molecular mechanism involved in the integration of LINE elements into host chromosomal DNA.

name: 5'-inverted insertion
description: A type of retrotransposed insertion structure characterized by an inverted 5' end.

name: 5'-truncated insertion
description: 5'-truncated insertion is a type of de novo LINE-1 insertion where the 5' end of the element is missing.

name: 5'-截短插入
description: 指新插入的LINE-1转座子序列在其5'端发生缺失，导致插入片段变短。

name: 5'-末端连接通路
description: LINE逆转录转座过程中，其5'端与宿主染色体DNA连接的不同分子机制，包括退火通路和直接通路。

name: 5'和3'端转导
description: LINE-1逆转录转座过程中，有时会捕获并携带其上游或下游的宿主基因组序列一同插入新位点。

name: 5'截断
description: LINE-1在插入基因组过程中，其5'端序列发生不完整整合的现象。

name: 5'端倒位
description: 在逆转录转座过程中，插入序列的5'端发生倒置，形成头对头（5'-inverted）的取向。

name: 5'端反向插入
description: LINE-1插入的一种结构类型，其5'端序列发生倒置。

name: 5'端截断
description: LINE-1在逆转录转座过程中，其5'端序列偶尔发生不完整复制。

name: 5'端截短
description: 绝大多数L1插入事件中，插入的L1序列在其5'端发生不完整拷贝的现象。

name: 5'端截短插入
description: LINE-1插入的一种结构类型，其5'端序列发生缺失。

name: 5'端整合
description: LINE逆转录转座过程中，其5'端DNA整合到宿主基因组的分子机制。

name: 5'端连接
description: 5'端连接是逆转录转座子整合到基因组时，其5'端与靶位点连接的机制。

name: 5'端额外核苷酸生成
description: 在LINE整合过程中，其5'端产生来源于侧翼序列的额外核苷酸的现象。

name: 5-methylcytosine-associated mutations (MAM)
description: Mutations, specifically C to T transitions, that are potentially associated with the deamination of 5-methylcytosine.

name: 5‘截短
description: LINE-1插入事件中，新生拷贝的5‘端发生截断的现象。

name: 5‘端附着
description: LINE-1插入过程中，新生拷贝的5‘端连接到染色体DNA的后续步骤。

name: A-tail expansion
description: The lengthening of the poly(A) tail of a retrotransposon, which in Alu elements occurs during insertion due to slippage by the L1 ORF2 protein.

name: A-tail扩展
description: 在插入过程中，由LINE-1 ORF2蛋白滑动导致的Alu元件A-tail序列长度增加。

name: A-to-I RNA editing
description: Adenosine-to-inosine editing of RNA, which can affect RNA structure and stability.

name: ADAR1 editing
description: ADAR1 editing refers to the adenosine-to-inosine RNA editing activity performed by ADAR1, which can alter RNA structure and sequence.

name: ADAR1介导的RNA编辑
description: ADAR1酶将RNA中的腺苷脱氨基为肌苷（A-to-I编辑）的过程。

name: ADAR1抑制LINE-1逆转录转座
description: ADAR1蛋白通过一种不依赖于其RNA编辑活性的机制，抑制LINE-1的逆转录转座活性。

name: AGO2-mediated repression
description: Gene repression mediated by Argonaute 2 (AGO2) targeting intronic L1 pre-mRNA complexes.

name: AKT signaling activation
description: AKT signaling activation is a beneficial pathway for cardiac regeneration.

name: ALKBH5抑制L1逆转录转座
description: m6A去甲基化酶ALKBH5通过去除L1 RNA的m6A修饰，从而抑制L1的逆转录转座活性。

name: AMPK activation
description: AMPK activation is the process of activating AMP-activated protein kinase, a master metabolic regulator, often in response to cellular stress.

name: AMPK signaling
description: AMPK signaling is a cellular energy-sensing pathway activated by exercise and metabolic stress.

name: AMPK激活
description: AMPK是一种主代谢调节因子，可由ROS、Ca2+水平升高或AMP/ATP比率增加等细胞应激信号激活。

name: AP-1 binding motif
description: AP-1 binding motif is a specific DNA sequence recognized and bound by the AP-1 transcription factor complex.

name: APOBEC3-mediated LINE-1 inhibition
description: APOBEC3-mediated LINE-1 inhibition refers to the restriction of LINE-1 retrotransposition by members of the APOBEC3 family of cytosine deaminases.

name: APOBEC3C介导的L1限制
description: APOBEC3C蛋白通过非脱氨酶依赖的机制抑制LINE-1的逆转录转座活性。

name: APOBEC3F介导的GA→AA突变
description: APOBEC3F等蛋白在逆转录元件基因组中诱导的特定G到A突变模式。

name: APOBEC3G介导的GG→AG突变
description: APOBEC3G蛋白在逆转录元件基因组中诱导的特定G到A突变模式。

name: APOBEC3酶催化胞嘧啶脱氨
description: APOBEC3家族酶在单链DNA底物上催化胞嘧啶脱氨基形成尿嘧啶的生化反应。

name: ARNT1-mediated L1 retrotransposition
description: A cellular machinery involving ARNT1 that mediates LINE-1 retrotransposition.

name: ASP hypomethylation
description: 反义启动子低甲基化，LINE-1反义启动子区域甲基化水平降低，导致其活性增强。

name: ASP-driven transcription of adjacent genes
description: The L1Hs antisense promoter drives the transcription of adjacent cellular genes.

name: ATM-MRN-SMC S phase checkpoint pathway
description: The ATM-MRN-SMC S phase checkpoint pathway is a specific DNA damage response pathway activated by double-strand breaks during the S phase of the cell cycle.

name: ATM-MRN-SMC S期检查点通路
description: ATM-MRN-SMC S期检查点通路是DNA双链断裂诱导的、在S期激活的DNA损伤应答通路。

name: ATM-signaling pathway activation
description: ATM信号通路激活，由DNA双链断裂触发，参与调控LINE-1逆转录转座。

name: ATM蛋白水平降低
description: ATM是一种关键的DNA损伤应答激酶，其蛋白稳态水平被β-HPV E6蛋白下调。

name: Aberrant RNA splicing
description: Aberrant RNA splicing is a process where errors in pre-mRNA splicing lead to the production of abnormal mRNA isoforms.

name: Aberrant epigenetic changes
description: Aberrant epigenetic changes refer to abnormal patterns of DNA methylation, histone modification, or chromatin structure that disrupt normal gene regulation.

name: Aberrant gene expression
description: Aberrant gene expression refers to abnormal levels or patterns of gene transcription.

name: Aberrant mRNA splicing
description: Aberrant mRNA splicing refers to the production of abnormal mRNA transcripts due to disruptions in the normal splicing process.

name: Aberrant proliferation
description: Aberrant proliferation refers to abnormal and uncontrolled cell division.

name: Aberrant recombination
description: Aberrant recombination refers to abnormal genetic recombination events, which can be facilitated by elements like LINE-1.

name: Aberrant splicing
description: Aberrant splicing refers to the formation of non-canonical RNA transcripts due to the use of alternative splice or polyadenylation signals.

name: Aberrant transcription
description: Aberrant transcription refers to abnormal gene expression patterns, such as decreased expression of specific isoforms.

name: Aberrant transcription start site (TSS) usage
description: Initiation of transcription from non-canonical or alternative start sites.

name: Aberrant upregulation
description: Aberrant upregulation refers to an abnormal increase in the expression level of a gene or genomic element.

name: Abnormal overexpression
description: Abnormal overexpression refers to the significantly increased expression level of a gene or element compared to normal conditions.

name: Abnormal splicing
description: Abnormal splicing is a disruption of the normal RNA splicing process, often leading to aberrant transcripts.

name: Accumulation
description: Accumulation refers to the process of gathering or increasing in number over time.

name: Accumulation of heritable L1 insertions
description: The accumulation of heritable L1 insertions refers to the build-up of LINE-1 insertions that are passed on to offspring.

name: Accumulation of somatic insertions
description: Accumulation of somatic insertions refers to the time-dependent increase in the number of new transposon insertions within the genomes of somatic cells.

name: Acquired chromosomal aberrations
description: Acquired chromosomal aberrations are structural or numerical changes in chromosomes that occur somatically, not inherited through the germline.

name: Acquisition of erythroid dominant expression
description: Acquisition of erythroid dominant expression refers to the evolutionary gain of tissue-specific (erythroid) gene expression patterns, potentially mediated by regulatory element insertion.

name: Activating p120-ctn/Rac1/Cdc42 axis
description: Triggering the activation of the Rho GTPase signaling pathway downstream of p120-catenin, involving Rac1 and Cdc42.

name: Activation
description: The process of becoming active or expressed, as observed for transposable elements in the blood of C9orf72 expansion carriers.

name: Activation of gene expression
description: The process of increasing the transcription of cellular genes.

name: Activation of mobile elements
description: Activation of mobile elements refers to the process by which transposons become transcriptionally and/or transpositionally active in somatic cells.

name: Activation of p120-ctn/Rac1/Cdc42 axis
description: LncMER52A triggers the activation of Rho GTPase downstream of p120-catenin, specifically the p120-ctn/Rac1/Cdc42 axis.

name: Activation of proinflammatory transposable elements
description: The process by which transposable elements are activated and contribute to an inflammatory state.

name: Activation of the cGAS-STING cascade
description: Activation of the cGAS-STING cascade is an innate immune response pathway triggered by cytoplasmic nucleic acids, such as those from transposable elements.

name: Activation of the host genome
description: The process of initiating transcriptional activity in the host genome during developmental stages like totipotency.

name: Activation of young LINE-1
description: The process by which young LINE-1 elements become transcriptionally active and potentially mobile.

name: Active L1 unit
description: The 'active L1 unit' refers to the actively transposable form of the LINE-1 element, which may retain features enabling recent transposition.

name: Active LINE-1 expression
description: Active LINE-1 expression refers to transcription initiated from the internal promoter of a LINE-1 element.

name: Active reverse transcriptase activity
description: Active reverse transcriptase activity refers to the enzymatic function that synthesizes DNA from an RNA template, essential for retrotransposition.

name: Adaptive evolution
description: Adaptive evolution is the process by which organisms become better suited to their environment through genetic changes.

name: Adoptive immunotherapy
description: Adoptive immunotherapy is a treatment that uses a patient's own immune cells, such as T cells, to fight disease.

name: Affect gene expression
description: LINE elements can modulate the transcription levels of human genes.

name: Affect neighboring gene expression
description: The process by which the activity of a genetic element influences the expression levels of genes located in its genomic vicinity.

name: Affect protein sequences
description: LINE elements can alter the amino acid sequence of proteins.

name: Affect splicing patterns
description: LINE elements can influence the alternative splicing of gene transcripts.

name: Affecting gene regulation
description: Influencing the control of when, where, and how much a gene is expressed.

name: Age estimation of retrotransposon subfamilies
description: Age estimation of retrotransposon subfamilies is a method to determine the time since a retrotransposon subfamily began proliferating in a genome.

name: Aging
description: Aging is the progressive decline in physiological function associated with the passage of time.

name: AhR signaling
description: AhR signaling is a pathway activated by ligands binding to the aryl hydrocarbon receptor, influencing gene expression.

name: AhR核转位蛋白-1 (ARNT1) 介导的细胞机制
description: 一种由ARNT1蛋白参与的细胞信号通路，能够介导LINE-1的逆转录转座活性。

name: Alteration of expression profile
description: Alteration of expression profile refers to changes in the pattern, level, or timing of gene expression.

name: Alteration of splicing
description: Alteration of splicing refers to changes in the process that removes introns and joins exons from pre-mRNA to form mature mRNA.

name: Alteration of target gene expression
description: Changes in the expression levels of genes, potentially caused by the insertion or activity of nearby elements like transposons.

name: Alteration of transcription initiation
description: Alteration of transcription initiation refers to changes in the process that determines where and when gene transcription begins.

name: Altered gene expression
description: Altered gene expression refers to changes in the transcription or splicing of a gene, potentially leading to abnormal transcript isoforms.

name: Altered gene transcription
description: Altered gene transcription refers to changes in the rate or pattern of gene expression.

name: Altered splicing
description: Altered splicing refers to changes in the RNA splicing patterns, which can be caused by transposon insertions.

name: Alternative RNA splicing at cryptic splice sites
description: Alternative RNA splicing at cryptic splice sites is a mechanism involved in SVA-mediated 5' transduction events.

name: Alternative endonuclease activity (alt-EN)
description: An endonuclease activity associated with the L1 ORF2p protein that is distinct from the canonical endonuclease domain activity and is evolutionarily conserved within the reverse transcriptase fold.

name: Alternative promoter
description: Alternative promoter refers to the use of different transcription start sites for a gene.

name: Alternative promoter function
description: L1 ASP can function as an alternative promoter for host genes, generating chimeric transcripts with coding regions identical to the host gene's ORF.

name: Alternative sense promoter activity
description: A potential promoter activity within the internal part of the LINE-1 5'-UTR that may drive the synthesis of a 5'-truncated mRNA.

name: Alternative splicing
description: Alternative splicing is a process that generates different mRNA isoforms from a single gene, with specific events altered in SETMAR knockout cell lines.

name: Alternative splicing and polyadenylation
description: Alternative splicing and polyadenylation is a mechanism that generates multiple RNA transcripts from a single gene locus.

name: Alternative transcriptional termination
description: Alternative transcriptional termination refers to the use of different transcription termination sites for a gene.

name: Alu RNA隔离
description: 高分子量APOBEC3G复合物通过将Alu RNA隔离在细胞质中，使其无法进入细胞核接触LINE-1的逆转录酶机制，从而抑制其逆转录转座。

name: Alu SINE 逆转录转座
description: 非自主的Alu SINE元件依赖LINE-1编码的蛋白（特别是ORF2p）进行扩增的过程。

name: Alu insertion
description: Alu insertion is the process by which Alu retrotransposons integrate into new genomic locations, often mediated by the LINE-1 machinery.

name: Alu insertion polymorphism
description: Alu insertion polymorphism refers to the presence or absence of an Alu element at a specific genomic locus, representing a form of genetic variation in human populations.

name: Alu mobilization
description: Alu mobilization is the process of Alu retrotransposition, which is driven by the LINE-1 ORF2 protein.

name: Alu recombination-mediated deletion (ARMD)
description: Alu recombination-mediated deletion is a genomic deletion event caused by recombination between Alu elements.

name: Alu replication cycle shunt
description: A proposed alternative pathway for Alu element propagation involving cytoplasmic cDNA synthesis, distinct from canonical retrotransposition.

name: Alu retrotransposition
description: Alu retrotransposition is the process by which Alu elements mobilize within the genome via an RNA intermediate.

name: Alu trans-mobilization
description: Alu trans-mobilization is the process by which the LINE-1 ORF2 protein machinery mobilizes non-autonomous Alu elements.

name: Alu transcription
description: Alu transcription is the process of generating RNA from the Alu promoter.

name: Alu-Alu recombination
description: Alu-Alu recombination is a form of unequal homologous recombination between Alu elements that can lead to genomic rearrangements.

name: Alu-induced mutagenesis
description: The process of mutation generation caused by the insertion or activity of Alu retrotransposons.

name: Alu-mediated deletion
description: Alu-mediated deletion is a genomic deletion event caused by recombination between Alu elements.

name: Alu-mediated recombination
description: Alu-mediated recombination is a genomic rearrangement process facilitated by homologous sequences between Alu elements.

name: Alu/Alu homologous recombination
description: A recombination event between homologous Alu elements, which can lead to genomic rearrangements.

name: Alu/L1逆转录转座
description: Alu和LINE-1转座子通过逆转录机制在基因组中移动的过程。

name: Alu/SVA retrotransposition
description: Alu/SVA逆转录转座是依赖LINE-1编码的蛋白机器（反式作用）完成的非自主逆转录转座过程。

name: Alu转座
description: 由LINE-1介导的Alu元件的逆转录转座过程。

name: Alu转座动员
description: LINE-1的ORF2蛋白能够反式辅助非自主的Alu逆转录转座子进行移动的过程。

name: Alu转录
description: Alu元件在特定条件下（如应激）被激活并产生RNA的过程。

name: Alu逆转录转座
description: Alu元件通过逆转录过程在基因组中移动的机制。

name: Amber suppression
description: Amber suppression is a technique that allows the incorporation of noncanonical amino acids into proteins by reassigning the amber stop codon (UAG) to code for an amino acid.

name: Amplification
description: Amplification refers to the process of increasing the copy number of a gene or DNA sequence.

name: Amplification burst
description: Amplification burst refers to a period of high activity and copy number increase for a transposable element family.

name: Ancestral recombination
description: Ancestral recombination refers to historical recombination events that have shaped the mosaic structure of haplotypes.

name: Aneuploidy
description: A condition characterized by an abnormal number of chromosomes in a cell.

name: Annealing pathway
description: The annealing pathway is one of the proposed mechanisms for joining the 5'-end of LINE elements to host chromosomal DNA during retrotransposition.

name: Antigen-antibody response
description: An immune response where antibodies bind to specific antigens, here demonstrated using LINE-1 proteins against antibodies in patient serum.

name: Antiretroelement activity
description: Antiretroelement activity refers to the ability of host proteins to inhibit the replication or mobilization of retroelements.

name: Antisense RNA production
description: L1 ASP can produce antisense RNAs complementary to host gene mRNAs.

name: Antisense transcript creation
description: The process of generating novel antisense transcripts, which can influence the sense partner gene function.

name: Antiviral immune activation
description: Antiviral immune activation refers to the host's immune response mechanisms triggered to combat viral infections.

name: Antiviral immune response
description: An antiviral immune response can be elicited by endogenous nucleic acid sensing pathways activated by LINE-1.

name: Antiviral response
description: The biological process by which the host organism defends against viral infections.

name: Arginine methylation
description: Arginine methylation is a post-translational modification where methyl groups are added to arginine residues in proteins.

name: Arms race model
description: An arms race model describes the evolutionary conflict between transposable elements and host defense mechanisms, such as zinc finger genes.

name: Assembly of RNA polymerase II complex
description: The formation of a multi-protein complex containing RNA polymerase II at a specific genomic locus to initiate transcription.

name: Asymmetric non-CG methylation
description: Asymmetric non-CG methylation refers to the maintenance of cytosine methylation at non-CG sites on one DNA strand but not the complementary strand.

name: Attenuation of anti-tumor immune response
description: Attenuation of anti-tumor immune response is a process where the suppression of certain repeat elements, like endogenous retrotransposons, weakens the body's immune attack against tumors.

name: Autoantibody generation
description: The production of antibodies (IgG, IgA) by the immune system that target self-antigens, such as transposon-encoded proteins.

name: Autophagy
description: Autophagy is a cellular degradation process that can target cytoplasmic components, including RNP complexes.

name: Autophagy activation
description: Autophagy activation is the initiation of the cellular process of degrading and recycling cellular components.

name: Bax activation
description: Bax activation is a pro-apoptotic event involving the conformational change and oligomerization of the Bax protein, leading to mitochondrial outer membrane permeabilization.

name: Biased genomic distribution
description: The non-homogeneous distribution of transposable elements, such as Alu elements, across different genomic regions like GC-rich and AT-rich isochores.

name: Bidirectional promoter activity
description: A regulatory function where a genomic locus, such as a transposable element, can initiate transcription in both directions.

name: Bidirectional promoter activity of L1-5'UTR
description: The 5' untranslated region (5'UTR) of L1 contains both sense and antisense promoters that can drive transcription.

name: Bidirectional transcription
description: Transcription that occurs in both directions from a common regulatory region.

name: Binding to ITRs
description: Binding to ITRs refers to the specific interaction between a transposase and the inverted terminal repeats (ITRs) of its cognate transposon.

name: Biological aging
description: Biological aging refers to the progressive decline in physiological function over time.

name: Biomolecular fluorescence complementation (BiFC)
description: BiFC is a technique to visualize protein-protein interactions by splitting a fluorescent protein and fusing the fragments to potential interacting partners.

name: Bipartite exon formation
description: Bipartite exon formation is the creation of a new exon from sequences derived from two separate introns, often facilitated by genomic rearrangements.

name: Bipartite regulatory element
description: A composite regulatory element consisting of a cAMP-inducible retroviral promoter (from the ERVWE1 5' LTR) and a cellular enhancer (the upstream regulatory element, URE).

name: Brain development
description: Brain development is the complex process by which the brain forms, grows, and matures, involving neural progenitor proliferation, differentiation, and migration.

name: Brain evolution
description: Brain evolution refers to the evolutionary changes and development of the brain structure and function across species.

name: Brain-specific L1 insertion
description: Brain-specific L1 insertion refers to the preferential insertion of LINE-1 elements into the genome of brain cells, particularly neurons.

name: Brain-specific genetic mosaicism
description: Brain-specific genetic mosaicism refers to increased genetic variation between neurons in the brain, partly due to somatic retrotransposition.

name: C-to-U editing
description: C-to-U editing is a deamination process converting cytosine to uracil in DNA or RNA, mediated by AID/APOBEC enzymes.

name: CAR-T cell construction
description: The process of engineering T cells to express chimeric antigen receptors (CARs) for cancer immunotherapy.

name: CAR-T cell manufacturing
description: CAR-T cell manufacturing is the process of producing chimeric antigen receptor T cells for therapeutic use.

name: CEBP binding motif
description: CEBP binding motif is a specific DNA sequence recognized and bound by the CEBP family of transcription factors.

name: CENP-A binding
description: CENP-A binding refers to the incorporation of the histone H3 variant CENP-A into chromatin, which is a hallmark of centromeric identity.

name: CNCC specification
description: Cranial neural crest cell (CNCC) specification is the process critical for vertebrate craniofacial development where cells acquire their specific identity and fate.

name: CREB activation
description: Activation of cAMP response element-binding protein (CREB) is a cellular signaling event that can regulate gene expression.

name: CRISPR interference (CRISPRi) silencing
description: A technique using a catalytically dead Cas9 protein to repress gene expression by blocking transcription.

name: CRISPR-Cas9-mediated gene activation (CRISPRa)
description: CRISPRa is a technique that uses a dCas9 fused to transcriptional activators to upregulate gene expression.

name: CRISPR-Cas9-mediated gene interference (CRISPRi)
description: CRISPRi is a technique that uses a catalytically dead Cas9 (dCas9) to repress gene expression by blocking transcription.

name: CRISPR/Cas9-induced DSB formation
description: CRISPR/Cas9-induced double-strand break (DSB) formation is a DNA damage mechanism triggered by the CRISPR/Cas9 genome editing tool.

name: CRISPR/Cas9-mediated integration
description: CRISPR/Cas9-mediated integration is a genome editing technique that uses the Cas9 nuclease guided by RNA to create targeted double-strand breaks, enabling precise insertion of DNA sequences.

name: CTCF-binding
description: CTCF-binding is a molecular mechanism where the CTCF protein binds to DNA, often involved in chromatin organization and gene regulation.

name: Cancer gene therapy
description: Cancer gene therapy involves the introduction of genetic material into cells to treat or prevent cancer.

name: Cancer genomic heterogeneity
description: Cancer genomic heterogeneity refers to the genetic diversity among cancer cells within a tumor, which can drive tumor evolution and therapy resistance.

name: Cap-dependent translation initiation
description: Cap-dependent translation initiation is a mechanism of protein synthesis initiation that requires a 5' cap structure on the mRNA.

name: Cardiomyocyte migration
description: The movement of heart muscle cells.

name: Catalysis by Mg2+ cation
description: The role of magnesium ions in facilitating the DNA cleavage reaction catalyzed by L1-Endonuclease.

name: Cell amplification
description: Cell amplification refers to the process of generating a large number of cells from a limited starting population.

name: Cell cycle alterations
description: Changes in the normal progression of the cell cycle, potentially linked to transposable element activity.

name: Cell death
description: Cell death is the process by which a cell ceases to function, which can be triggered by various stresses including energy depletion.

name: Cell engineering
description: Cell engineering involves the genetic modification of cells to confer new functions or properties.

name: Cell fate control
description: The process that determines the developmental pathway and final identity of a cell.

name: Cell migration
description: Cell migration is the movement of cells, a process critical for cancer invasion and metastasis.

name: Cell reprogramming
description: Cell reprogramming is the process of converting somatic cells into induced pluripotent stem cells.

name: Cell senescence
description: Cell senescence is a state of stable cell cycle arrest that can act as a tumor-protective mechanism.

name: Cell toxicity
description: A process that leads to damage or death of cells.

name: Cell type specific expression
description: Cell type specific expression refers to the pattern of gene expression that is restricted to particular cell types.

name: Cell type-specific transcription termination
description: Cell type-specific transcription termination refers to termination events that occur selectively in particular cell types.

name: Cell-cell fusion
description: Cell-cell fusion is the process by which individual cells merge to form a multinucleated syncytium.

name: Cell-type-independent expression
description: Gene or transposon expression that occurs across different cell types without restriction.

name: Cellular aging
description: Cellular aging refers to the progressive decline in cellular function and viability over time.

name: Cellular damage
description: Damage to cellular components or functions, which can be associated with retrotransposition.

name: Cellular phosphorylation regulatory cascades
description: Cellular phosphorylation regulatory cascades are pathways that L1 activity is dependent on and integrated with.

name: Cellular plasticity
description: Cellular plasticity refers to the ability of a cell to change its phenotype, such as during processes like epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition (EMT).

name: Cellular regulation of retrotransposition
description: The mechanisms by which host cells control or modulate the activity of LINE-1 retrotransposition.

name: Cellular reprogramming
description: Cellular reprogramming is the process of converting differentiated cells into induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs).

name: Cellular senescence
description: A state of irreversible cell cycle arrest, often associated with aging and tissue dysfunction.

name: Cellular stress response
description: Cellular stress response refers to the changes in gene expression patterns when cells are exposed to various stress conditions, such as hypoxia, oxidative stress, or UV stress.

name: Centromere evolution
description: Centromere evolution refers to the processes that shape the structure and function of centromeric DNA sequences over time.

name: Centromere function disruption
description: Centromere function disruption refers to the impairment or loss of the centromere's ability to ensure proper chromosome segregation.

name: Centromere remodeling
description: Centromere remodeling refers to structural and functional changes in the centromeric region of a chromosome.

name: Centrosome amplification
description: Centrosome amplification is the abnormal increase in the number of centrosomes, which can lead to chromosome missegregation and genomic instability.

name: Chimera formation
description: Chimera formation refers to the generation of hybrid sequences between transposons and other genomic elements.

name: Chimeric L1 formation
description: Chimeric L1 formation is a process resulting in a hybrid L1 element containing sequences from both an endogenous L1 and an engineered L1.

name: Chimeric antigen receptor T cell generation
description: Chimeric antigen receptor T cell generation is the process of engineering T cells to express a synthetic receptor that targets specific antigens on cancer cells.

name: Chimeric gene creation
description: Chimeric gene creation is the formation of a new gene from parts of distinct genes, often through mechanisms like retrotransposition-mediated exon shuffling.

name: Chimeric insertion formation
description: The process where LINE-1 forms hybrid insertions with non-coding RNAs and mRNAs.

name: Chimeric protein formation
description: Chimeric protein formation refers to the creation of a novel protein by the fusion of coding sequences from different genes or genetic elements.

name: Chimeric retrogene formation
description: Chimeric retrogene formation is the process of creating bipartite elements composed of DNA copies of cellular transcripts fused to each other or to the 3' part of a LINE retrotransposon.

name: Chimeric retrotranscript formation
description: Chimeric retrotranscript formation is the process of generating fused DNA copies of different RNAs, including mRNAs, through RNA-RNA recombination during L1 reverse transcription.

name: Chimeric retrotransposon formation
description: The process by which a composite retrotransposon is created through the fusion of distinct genetic elements, such as a non-coding RNA and a truncated retrotransposon.

name: Chimeric transcript formation
description: The generation of a hybrid RNA molecule from the fusion of sequences from different genomic loci, such as a transposon and a host gene.

name: Cholesterol ester accumulation
description: Cholesterol ester accumulation is the buildup of cholesterol esters within cells.

name: Chromatin accessibility for LTR expression
description: Chromatin accessibility is crucial for the expression of LTR-derived transcripts like lncMER52A.

name: Chromatin accessibility landscape regulation
description: The process of modulating the openness or compaction of chromatin, which influences gene expression.

name: Chromatin binding
description: Chromatin binding is the process by which proteins specifically associate with chromatin, the complex of DNA and proteins in the nucleus.

name: Chromatin changes in aging
description: Global and local chromatin changes associated with human aging.

name: Chromatin looping mediated by homologous Alu elements
description: Chromatin looping mediated by homologous Alu elements is a process that brings distal DNA regions into close proximity.

name: Chromatin remodeling
description: Chromatin remodeling is the dynamic modification of chromatin architecture to allow access of condensed genomic DNA to the regulatory transcription machinery proteins.

name: Chromatin unfolding
description: Chromatin unfolding refers to the decompaction or increased accessibility of chromatin structure.

name: Chromatinization
description: The dynamic epigenetic process of DNA being incorporated into nucleosomes, forming chromatin.

name: Chromosomal distribution
description: Chromosomal distribution describes the pattern of how transposon insertions are spread across different chromosomes.

name: Chromosomal inversion
description: Chromosomal inversion is a structural rearrangement where a segment of a chromosome is reversed end to end.

name: Chromosomal rearrangement
description: Chromosomal rearrangement is a structural change in chromosomes.

name: Chromosomal translocation
description: Chromosomal translocation is a structural rearrangement where a segment of one chromosome is transferred to another chromosome.

name: Chromosome instability
description: Chromosome instability refers to a high rate of chromosome missegregation and structural rearrangements, a hallmark of many cancers.

name: Chromosome rearrangements
description: Chromosome rearrangements are structural changes in chromosomes, which can be caused by events like recombination between transposons.

name: Chromosome segregation
description: Chromosome segregation is the process during cell division where sister chromatids or homologous chromosomes are separated and distributed into daughter cells.

name: Chromothripsis
description: A catastrophic cellular event involving massive, clustered genomic rearrangements confined to one or a few chromosomes.

name: Chronic marrow inflammation
description: Chronic marrow inflammation is a persistent inflammatory state within the bone marrow niche, often driven by immune dysregulation and cytokine secretion.

name: Circular RNA formation
description: Circular RNA formation is a process where RNA molecules form covalently closed loops.

name: Cis preference
description: Cis preference is the concept that the proteins encoded by a particular L1 preferentially act upon their own encoding RNA.

name: Cis-enhancer supply
description: Cis-enhancer supply refers to the provision of a regulatory element that increases gene expression from the same DNA molecule.

name: Cis-preferential reverse transcription
description: The process where the LINE-1 reverse transcriptase (ORF2p) preferentially uses its own encoding RNA as a template for reverse transcription.

name: Cis-regulatory element acquisition
description: Cis-regulatory element acquisition is the evolutionary process of gaining DNA sequences that regulate nearby gene expression.

name: Cleavage
description: Cleavage refers to the enzymatic cutting of DNA strands, such as at the ends of a transposon during transposition.

name: Cleavage-stage transcriptional program
description: Cleavage-stage transcriptional program refers to the specific set of genes and elements activated during the early cleavage stages of embryonic development.

name: Clonal expansion
description: Clonal expansion is the process by which a cell with a specific mutation gives rise to a population of identical daughter cells.

name: Cluster-independent piRNA production
description: A mode of piRNA production that targets young TEs without involving piRNA clusters, present at both prenatal and postnatal stages of spermatogenesis.

name: Clustered transposon insertion
description: Clustered transposon insertion is the phenomenon where multiple transposon insertions occur in close genomic proximity.

name: Co-evolution
description: Co-evolution refers to the process where two or more entities, such as LTRs and the pol gene, evolve together over time.

name: Co-integration
description: Co-integration is the process where two or more distinct DNA sequences are integrated together into a new genomic location.

name: Co-option
description: Co-option is the evolutionary process by which a transposable element is recruited for a new function, such as gene regulation.

name: Co-repression of genes
description: Co-repression of genes refers to the joint silencing of genes by multiple factors.

name: Coating the RNA
description: Coating the RNA is a proposed role for the ORF1 protein (ORF1p) during L1 retrotransposition.

name: Coclustering
description: Coclustering refers to the tendency of most Alu elements to be located near each other in the genome.

name: Cognitive function
description: Cognitive function refers to mental processes in the brain, including attention, impulsivity, and working memory.

name: Coiled-coil domain stability and dynamics
description: The structural integrity and conformational flexibility of the three-stranded coiled-coil (3SCC) domain in ORF1p.

name: Colocalization with immune response genes
description: The genomic positioning of HERV and MaLR loci near genes that are involved and modulated in the immune response.

name: Colonization of the human genome by mobile genetic elements
description: The process by which mobile genetic elements, such as retrotransposons, accumulate and occupy a significant portion of the human genome.

name: Compartmentalization of translation
description: The spatial organization of protein synthesis, such as translation on free cytoplasmic ribosomes versus membrane-bound ribosomes.

name: Competitive recruitment of transcription factors
description: A process where a DNA element binds and sequesters transcription factors, affecting their availability at other genomic sites.

name: Competitive relationships between TEs
description: Interactions where different transposable elements may compete for host resources or regulatory systems.

name: Complementation in trans
description: Complementation in trans is a copying mechanism where elements are copied by other elements of the same family.

name: Complex assembly
description: Complex assembly is a divalent metal ion-dependent step involving transposase and the transposon end(s) prior to the first catalytic step.

name: Concerted evolution
description: Concerted evolution is a process that maintains sequence homogeneity among members of a multigene family, such as the RNU2 locus.

name: Conditional knockout
description: Conditional knockout is a method to inactivate a gene in a specific tissue or at a specific time.

name: Conformational change
description: A significant conformational change between first- and second-strand cleavage at each transposon end is proposed for mariner excision.

name: Conservation
description: The evolutionary process by which a genetic feature is maintained in a population due to selective pressure.

name: Conserved expression
description: Conserved expression refers to the pattern of gene or transcript expression that is maintained across different species.

name: Constitutive splicing
description: Constitutive splicing refers to exons that are always included in the mature mRNA transcript.

name: Contact domain boundary formation
description: The creation of boundaries between topologically associating domains (TADs) in the genome.

name: Contribute coding sequences
description: The process by which retrotransposons provide protein-coding sequences to the genome.

name: Contribute regulatory sequences
description: The process by which retrotransposons provide regulatory elements (e.g., promoters, enhancers) to the genome.

name: Contribute to phenotypic heterogeneity
description: SINEs can serve as beneficial building blocks that contribute to diversity in observable traits.

name: Contributing to regulatory elements near quantitative trait loci (QTLs)
description: The function of being part of genomic elements that regulate gene expression and are located near loci associated with quantitative traits.

name: Convergent evolution of gene expression
description: The independent evolution of similar gene expression patterns in different lineages through distinct molecular mechanisms.

name: Converging transcription of antisense-oriented genes
description: A mechanism contributing to the origin of genic postnatal prepachytene piRNA clusters.

name: Copper(I) binding
description: The process by which the ORF1p coiled-coil domain binds copper(I) ions with high affinity.

name: Copy number alterations
description: Changes in the number of copies of a specific genomic region, such as aneuploidies.

name: Copy number variations
description: Copy number variations are a type of structural variation involving changes in the number of copies of a particular genomic region.

name: Copying by non-HERV machinery
description: Copying by non-HERV machinery is a mechanism where HERV elements are copied by cellular machinery not specific to HERVs.

name: Core decidual regulatory network
description: The core decidual regulatory network is a set of molecular interactions and pathways that govern the decidualization process in endometrial stromal cells.

name: Coretropose
description: Coretropose is the process by which LINE-1 elements mobilize and integrate various non-autonomous RNAs into the genome.

name: Cotranscription
description: Cotranscription refers to the simultaneous transcription of two or more genetic elements from a shared promoter or regulatory region.

name: CpA to TpA mutation
description: A specific type of 5-methylcytosine-associated mutation.

name: CpG DNA methylation
description: CpG DNA methylation is an epigenetic modification primarily occurring at CpG dinucleotides, crucial for silencing transposons.

name: CpG dinucleotide mutation
description: CpG dinucleotides are hypermutable sites in the genome, often undergoing cytosine methylation and subsequent deamination.

name: CpG hypomethylation
description: CpG hypomethylation refers to a decrease in the methylation level of cytosine nucleotides in CpG dinucleotide contexts, often associated with genomic instability and altered gene expression.

name: CpG island creation
description: The process by which retrotransposition events lead to the formation of new CpG islands in the genome.

name: CpG island hypermethylation
description: CpG island hypermethylation is an epigenetic modification involving the addition of methyl groups to cytosine residues in CpG-rich regions, often associated with gene silencing.

name: CpG methylation
description: CpG methylation is an epigenetic modification involving the addition of a methyl group to the cytosine nucleotide in a CpG dinucleotide, often associated with gene silencing.

name: CpG methylation in L1 promoter
description: DNA methylation at CpG sites within the promoter region of LINE-1 elements, which regulates their expression.

name: CpG methylation-mediated repression
description: CpG甲基化介导的抑制是通过DNA甲基化来沉默转座子等重复元件表达的表观遗传机制。

name: CpG mutation
description: CpG mutation refers to the high mutation rate of CpG dinucleotides, often resulting in their conversion to TpG or CpA.

name: CpG to TpG mutation
description: A specific type of 5-methylcytosine-associated mutation.

name: CpT to TpT mutation
description: A specific type of 5-methylcytosine-associated mutation.

name: Creation of chimeric retrotranscripts
description: The process of generating fused retrotranscripts, such as U6-L1, using the L1 integration machinery.

name: Creation of new microsatellites
description: The creation of new microsatellites is a genomic consequence of L1 insertions.

name: Cryptic exon activation
description: Cryptic exon activation refers to the inclusion of a normally non-coding intronic sequence into a mature mRNA transcript due to the creation of a new splice site.

name: Cryptic exon inclusion
description: Cryptic exon inclusion is a splicing abnormality where a normally non-coding intronic sequence is incorporated into the mature mRNA.

name: Cryptic polyadenylation
description: Cryptic polyadenylation refers to the use of non-canonical or suboptimal polyadenylation signals within a transcript, leading to premature termination of transcription.

name: Cryptic promoter activity
description: Cryptic promoter activity refers to the initiation of transcription from sequences not normally functioning as promoters, which can be activated by nearby genomic alterations.

name: Cryptic splice site activation
description: Cryptic splice site activation refers to the unmasking and utilization of normally silent splice sites within introns or exons, often due to mutations.

name: Cryptic splicing variation
description: Cryptic splicing variation refers to alternative splicing events caused by sequence variation within intronic regions, such as VNTRs.

name: Cytidine deamination
description: An enzymatic process that converts cytidine to uridine, which can be a restriction mechanism against retrotransposons.

name: Cytoplasmic DNA accumulation
description: Cytoplasmic DNA accumulation refers to the buildup of DNA, including from active LINE-1 elements, in the cell cytoplasm.

name: Cytoplasmic accumulation of RNA-DNA hybrids
description: Cytoplasmic accumulation of RNA-DNA hybrids is a process where RNA-DNA hybrid molecules derived from retrotransposons accumulate in the cytoplasm.

name: Cytoplasmic aggregation
description: The formation of protein aggregates in the cytoplasm, a feature of the LINE-1 ORF1p protein.

name: Cytoplasmic expression of ORF1p/ORF2p
description: The localization of LINE-1 encoded ORF1p and ORF2p proteins within the cell cytoplasm.

name: Cytoplasmic foci localization
description: Cytoplasmic foci localization is the process where L1-encoded proteins and L1 RNA accumulate in cytoplasmic foci, often associated with stress granules.

name: Cytoplasmic sequestration
description: Cytoplasmic sequestration refers to the process of trapping or confining molecules, such as RNA, in the cytoplasm away from their site of action.

name: Cytosine methylation
description: Cytosine methylation is an epigenetic modification involving the addition of a methyl group to cytosine residues in DNA, often associated with gene silencing.

name: Cytosine methylation of L1 promoters
description: A cellular mechanism involving high-density cytosine methylation to downregulate the expression of L1 proteins.

name: Cytosolic ribonuclease (RNase) degradation
description: Cytosolic ribonuclease degradation is a pathway for degrading LINE-1 RNA, identified as a major regulatory mechanism in the model.

name: Cytotoxicity
description: Cytotoxicity refers to the quality of being toxic to cells, causing cell damage or death.

name: DICER-dependent destruction of L1 mRNAs
description: A cellular mechanism involving DICER to downregulate the expression of L1 proteins by destroying L1 mRNAs.

name: DICER依赖性mRNA降解
description: 细胞通过DICER依赖性途径破坏LINE-1 mRNA来下调其蛋白表达。

name: DNA 5-methylcytosine (5mC) methylation
description: DNA 5-methylcytosine (5mC) methylation is an epigenetic modification involved in the silencing of transposable elements.

name: DNA IR损伤和ATR介导的细胞反应
description: 细胞对电离辐射（IR）引起的DNA损伤并通过ATR激酶通路作出反应的过程。

name: DNA bending and minor groove widening
description: A structural mechanism induced by the βB6-βB5 hairpin loop of L1-Endonuclease, which protrudes into the DNA minor groove, causing bending and widening to position the scissile bond for cleavage.

name: DNA binding
description: DNA binding is the molecular function of proteins or other molecules to attach to specific sequences or structures of DNA.

name: DNA binding site spread
description: The spread of DNA binding sites refers to their distribution across the genome through mechanisms such as transposon mobilization.

name: DNA break
description: DNA断裂，由L1编码的内切酶在整合过程中产生。

name: DNA break repair via homology-dependent pathway
description: A pathway for repairing DNA double-strand breaks that uses a homologous DNA sequence as a template, such as homologous recombination.

name: DNA break repair via nonhomologous end joining
description: A pathway for repairing DNA double-strand breaks by directly ligating the broken ends, often resulting in small insertions or deletions.

name: DNA breaks
description: DNA breaks are physical discontinuities in the DNA backbone, which can be single-strand or double-strand breaks.

name: DNA cleavage
description: DNA cleavage is the enzymatic process of breaking phosphodiester bonds in DNA.

name: DNA cleavage activity
description: DNA cleavage activity refers to the enzymatic ability to cut DNA strands.

name: DNA cleavage by L1 endonuclease
description: DNA cleavage specificity by the L1-encoded endonuclease primarily dictates sites of L1 insertion.

name: DNA clustering
description: DNA clustering refers to the phenomenon where specific genome elements are non-randomly grouped together in the genome.

name: DNA crosslink repair
description: DNA crosslink repair is a pathway that resolves interstrand crosslinks, and the Fanconi anemia pathway is a key component of this process.

name: DNA damage
description: DNA damage refers to alterations in the chemical structure of DNA, such as breaks or modifications.

name: DNA damage accumulation
description: DNA damage accumulation refers to the increase in genomic lesions.

name: DNA damage repair
description: DNA damage repair is a cellular process that identifies and corrects damage to DNA molecules to maintain genome integrity.

name: DNA damage response
description: DNA损伤应答，是细胞感知和修复DNA损伤的机制。

name: DNA damage signaling
description: DNA damage signaling refers to the cellular pathways that detect and respond to DNA lesions, such as double-strand breaks.

name: DNA damage signaling pathway activation
description: DNA damage signaling pathway activation is the cellular response triggered by DNA lesions, such as double-strand breaks, leading to the recruitment of repair proteins.

name: DNA damage-mediated programmed cell death
description: DNA damage-mediated programmed cell death is a cell death pathway triggered by genomic damage, such as that potentially caused by retrotransposon activity.

name: DNA deamination-independent inhibition
description: APOBEC3B和APOBEC3F通过不依赖DNA脱氨的机制抑制LINE-1逆转录转座。

name: DNA deamination-independent mechanism
description: A mechanism of L1 inhibition that does not involve cytidine deamination.

name: DNA deletions
description: DNA缺失，指转座子活动过程中或活动后导致的基因组DNA片段丢失。

name: DNA demethylation
description: DNA demethylation is an active process of removing methyl groups from cytosine bases in DNA, often mediated by enzymes like TET.

name: DNA double strand breaks (DNA DSBs)
description: DNA double strand breaks (DNA DSBs) are a type of severe DNA damage where both strands of the DNA helix are severed.

name: DNA double-strand break (DSB) formation
description: DNA double-strand break formation is a type of DNA damage involving breaks in both strands of the DNA double helix.

name: DNA double-strand break (DSB) repair
description: DNA double-strand break repair is a cellular mechanism to fix breaks in both strands of the DNA molecule.

name: DNA double-strand break repair
description: A cellular process for repairing DNA double-strand breaks, which may be utilized by endonuclease-independent L1 insertions.

name: DNA double-strand break repair by non-homologous end joining
description: Non-homologous end joining (NHEJ) is a pathway that repairs double-strand breaks in DNA by directly ligating the broken ends together.

name: DNA double-strand breaks
description: DNA双链断裂，由LINE-1内切酶活性在插入位点诱导产生。

name: DNA double-strand breaks (DSBs)
description: DNA double-strand breaks are a form of severe DNA damage where both strands of the DNA helix are severed.

name: DNA double-stranded breaks
description: A type of DNA damage where both strands of the DNA double helix are severed.

name: DNA duplication
description: DNA duplication is a process that occurs more frequently in Alu- and GC-rich chromosomal regions.

name: DNA elongation
description: The process of extending a DNA strand by adding nucleotides, a key step in reverse transcription.

name: DNA gain
description: DNA gain refers to the accumulation of non-coding DNA sequences, including transposable elements, in the genome.

name: DNA hypomethylation
description: A reduction in the level of DNA methylation, often associated with increased transcriptional activity of repetitive elements.

name: DNA loss
description: DNA loss refers to the removal of non-coding DNA sequences from the genome.

name: DNA melting
description: DNA melting refers to the local unwinding or separation of the DNA double helix.

name: DNA methylation
description: An epigenetic modification that represses transcription, including that of transposable elements.

name: DNA methylation analysis
description: DNA methylation analysis is a method to assess the methylation status of cytosine residues in DNA, often at CpG sites.

name: DNA methylation changes
description: DNA methylation changes refer to alterations in the pattern of DNA methylation, which is an epigenetic modification.

name: DNA methylation pattern redistribution
description: DNA methylation pattern redistribution refers to changes in the distribution of methylated cytosines across the genome or within specific genomic elements.

name: DNA methylation variation
description: DNA methylation variation refers to differences in methylation levels between individuals or conditions.

name: DNA methylation-based repression
description: A repression mechanism involving DNA methyltransferases that silences transposon expression by adding methyl groups to DNA.

name: DNA nicking
description: DNA nicking is the process of cleaving one strand of a DNA duplex, which is a critical step in LINE-1 retrotransposition.

name: DNA polymerization
description: DNA polymerization refers to the synthesis of DNA strands, specifically catalyzed by the L1 reverse transcriptase (L1 RT) on its own L1 RNA template in vitro.

name: DNA quantitation
description: DNA quantitation is the process of determining the amount of amplifiable DNA in a biological sample.

name: DNA recognition and binding
description: DNA recognition and binding is the process by which a protein specifically interacts with a DNA sequence.

name: DNA recognition by L1 endonuclease
description: The physical basis of L1 endonuclease DNA recognition, which is specific for unusual DNA structural features at TpA junctions of A-tracts.

name: DNA recombination
description: DNA recombination is the process of breaking and rejoining DNA strands to produce new combinations of genetic material.

name: DNA release
description: DNA release refers to the process by which DNA is released from cells into the extracellular space, such as into plasma.

name: DNA repair
description: DNA repair is a collection of processes by which a cell identifies and corrects damage to the DNA molecules that encode its genome.

name: DNA repair response
description: DNA repair response is a cellular mechanism that corrects DNA damage.

name: DNA replication
description: DNA replication is the process of copying a cell's DNA to produce two identical DNA molecules.

name: DNA replication and repair
description: A recurring theme for retrotransposons is their tie-in to DNA replication and repair processes within the host cell.

name: DNA replication restart
description: A cellular process involving specific proteins that reinitiate DNA synthesis at stalled or collapsed replication forks.

name: DNA sequence-specific recruitment
description: A recruitment mechanism where a protein complex is targeted to specific genomic loci based on recognition of particular DNA sequences.

name: DNA strand breaks
description: The occurrence of breaks in the DNA double helix.

name: DNA strand breaks with incomplete retrotransposition
description: 伴随不完全逆转录转座的DNA链断裂，逆转录酶抑制剂可能通过此机制诱导细胞周期阻滞和免疫反应。

name: DNA target recognition
description: DNA target recognition refers to the process by which mobile genetic elements identify specific sequences or structures in the genome for insertion.

name: DNA target site recognition
description: The process by which the LINE-1 endonuclease identifies and binds to specific DNA sequences for nicking.

name: DNA transposition
description: DNA transposition is the movement of DNA transposons within the genome, typically involving a 'cut-and-paste' mechanism.

name: DNA transposon activity
description: DNA transposon activity refers to the mobilization of DNA transposons via a 'cut-and-paste' mechanism.

name: DNA-binding and looping activity
description: DNA-binding and looping activity refers to the ability of a protein to bind DNA and bring distant sites together, forming a loop.

name: DNA-directed polymerization
description: DNA指导的聚合是ORF2p以DNA为模板合成DNA的能力。

name: DNA:RNA heteroduplex formation
description: DNA:RNA heteroduplex formation is a molecular structure consisting of one strand of DNA and one strand of RNA, which can be an intermediate or product of reverse transcription.

name: DNA:RNA hybrid formation
description: DNA:RNA hybrid formation refers to the generation of structures where RNA molecules anneal to complementary DNA sequences.

name: DNA低甲基化
description: DNA序列中胞嘧啶甲基化水平降低的现象，在癌症中常见。

name: DNA修复
description: 细胞修复DNA损伤的过程。

name: DNA修复抑制
description: 重金属通过置换细胞蛋白中的Mg和Zn，抑制DNA修复蛋白或其他酶的活性。

name: DNA修复机制
description: DNA修复机制参与处理DNA损伤，可能影响L1的移动。

name: DNA修复活性降低
description: 细胞修复DNA损伤的能力减弱，可能影响逆转录转座的下游事件。

name: DNA修复蛋白募集
description: DNA损伤信号通路激活后，DNA修复蛋白被招募到损伤位点的过程。

name: DNA去甲基化
description: TET酶通过氧化5-甲基胞嘧啶介导的DNA主动去甲基化过程。

name: DNA双链断裂
description: 基因组DNA两条链同时断裂的损伤形式。

name: DNA双链断裂修复
description: 一种DNA损伤修复过程，该过程可能与内切核酸酶非依赖性LINE-1插入有关。

name: DNA双链断裂形成
description: DNA双链断裂是DNA损伤的一种严重形式，可由多种因素（包括转座子活动）引发。

name: DNA弯曲
description: L1内切酶与DNA结合后诱导DNA发生弯曲，导致DNA小沟变宽，从而将可切割的磷酸二酯键置于催化位点。

name: DNA损伤
description: 由环境因素（如重金属）或转座子激活引起的DNA结构破坏。

name: DNA损伤信号通路激活
description: 由LINE-1插入诱导的DNA双链断裂所触发的细胞反应。

name: DNA损伤修复
description: 摘要中提及，MILs偏好位于具有此关键功能的长基因中。

name: DNA损伤反应
description: 细胞对DNA损伤的应答机制，在本文中由LINE-1再激活所诱导。

name: DNA损伤应答
description: DNA损伤应答是细胞感知和修复DNA损伤的一系列信号通路。

name: DNA甲基化
description: 一种表观遗传修饰机制，通过向DNA添加甲基基团来调控基因表达。

name: DNA甲基化介导的启动子抑制
description: DNA甲基化是介导LINE-1启动子沉默和抑制其活性的主要表观遗传机制。

name: DNA甲基化抑制
description: 通过DNA甲基化修饰来抑制LINE-1等转座子的活性。

name: DNA甲基化改变
description: 在精神疾病患者前额叶皮层中发现的L1启动子区域的DNA甲基化水平差异。

name: DNA甲基化缺失
description: DNA分子上甲基基团的丢失，通常导致基因或转座子表观遗传抑制的解除。

name: DNA第二链切口
description: 在第一链逆转录过程中，DNA第二链被切割的现象。

name: DNA结合活性
description: 当RNA被ORF1p过饱和时，L1 RNP凝聚物获得结合DNA的能力。

name: DNA编辑
description: 由AID/APOBEC蛋白介导的DNA序列改变，如G到A突变。

name: DNA羟甲基化
description: 一种DNA修饰，与DNA甲基化一同被用于鉴定表观遗传活跃的L1。

name: DNA聚合
description: LINE-1逆转录酶在体外以其自身模板催化DNA依赖或RNA依赖的DNA合成。

name: DNA脱氨基
description: APOBEC3蛋白家族成员通过将胞嘧啶脱氨基为尿嘧啶，从而编辑DNA或RNA的机制。

name: DNA识别
description: L1内切酶通过识别DNA的物理结构特征（如TpA连接处的特殊构象）来结合底物。

name: DNA转导
description: LINE-1逆转录转座过程中，其下游的独特DNA片段被异常地作为转座事件的一部分而移动的机制。

name: DNA靶向与切割
description: LINE-1的ORF2p蛋白识别并切割特定DNA位点的过程。

name: De novo DNA methylation
description: De novo DNA methylation is the process of establishing new DNA methylation marks, often at transposable elements, to repress their transcription.

name: De novo insertion
description: De novo insertion refers to the integration of a transposon into a new genomic location.

name: De novo mutation
description: De novo mutation refers to a new genetic alteration that is present for the first time in a family member, caused by an error in DNA replication or repair.

name: De novo repression
description: De novo repression refers to the initial silencing of genetic elements.

name: De novo retrotransposition
description: De novo retrotransposition is the process by which a retrotransposon generates a new genomic insertion through reverse transcription and integration.

name: De novo transposon insertion
description: De novo transposon insertion refers to a new insertion event of a transposon into the genome that was not present in the parental genomes.

name: De-differentiation
description: De-differentiation is a cellular process where specialized cells revert to a less specialized state.

name: De-repression
description: De-repression is the process by which silenced transposable elements become active or accessible.

name: Decidualization
description: Decidualization is the process of endometrial stromal cell differentiation, a key event in establishing endometrial receptivity for embryo implantation.

name: Decrease of RNA levels
description: Decrease of RNA levels refers to a reduction in the amount of specific RNA transcripts.

name: Decreased gene expression
description: Decreased gene expression refers to a reduction in the level of transcription or translation of a specific gene.

name: Decreased global DNA methylation
description: Decreased global DNA methylation is an epigenetic state characterized by a reduction in the overall level of DNA methylation across the genome.

name: Decreased transcription
description: Decreased transcription refers to a reduction in the rate or level of gene transcription.

name: Defense mechanism against transposons
description: A biological process, such as DNA methylation, that suppresses the activity or deleterious effects of transposable elements.

name: Degradation assessment
description: Degradation assessment is the qualitative measure of the extent of DNA degradation in a sample.

name: Deletion
description: Deletion is the process by which a transposable element is removed from a genomic location.

name: Depletion of mRNA and protein levels
description: Depletion of mRNA and protein levels refers to a significant reduction in the quantity of specific mRNA transcripts and their corresponding proteins.

name: Derepression of retrotransposons
description: The process by which retrotransposons, such as LINE-1, are released from transcriptional silencing and become active.

name: Derepression of transposable elements
description: Derepression of transposable elements refers to the loss of normal silencing mechanisms, leading to increased expression and potential activity of these elements.

name: Determination of chronological order and relative ages
description: A method to infer the chronological order and relative ages of human transposable elements spanning over 100 million years based on their insertion/fragmentation patterns.

name: Developmental control
description: Developmental control refers to the regulation of processes that guide the growth and differentiation of an organism from an embryo.

name: Developmental program repression
description: The process of suppressing or silencing gene expression programs associated with earlier stages of development.

name: Developmental regulation
description: The control of processes during organism development.

name: Dicer-mediated RNA interference (RNAi) silencing
description: Dicer-mediated RNA interference (RNAi) silencing is a cellular defense mechanism that uses small interfering RNAs to limit the expansion of retrotransposons.

name: Dicer介导的RNAi沉默
description: Dicer介导的RNAi沉默是一种通过Dicer酶产生小干扰RNA来抑制基因组寄生虫（如逆转录转座子）活性的防御机制。

name: Dicistronic structure
description: A dicistronic structure refers to an mRNA that contains two open reading frames (ORFs) that can be translated into two separate proteins.

name: Differential expression
description: Differential expression refers to the condition where a gene or element is expressed at different levels between two or more conditions, such as tumor versus healthy tissue.

name: Differential expression of miRNAs
description: Differential expression of miRNAs, such as the let-7 family, involves changes in their levels, which can regulate genes involved in EMT and metastasis.

name: Differential gene expression
description: Differential gene expression refers to changes in the level of gene transcription, as identified in SETMAR knockout cell lines.

name: Differential methylation
description: Differential methylation refers to differences in DNA methylation patterns, such as between alleles or genomic regions.

name: Differentiation
description: Differentiation is the process by which pluripotent stem cells develop into specialized cell types.

name: Differentiation of sex chromosomes
description: Differentiation of sex chromosomes refers to the evolutionary process where homologous chromosomes (X and Y) accumulate differences and stop recombining over most of their length.

name: Differentiation repression
description: Differentiation repression is a process that maintains pluripotency by inhibiting the transition of stem cells into specialized cell types.

name: Direct occupation
description: Direct occupation refers to transcription factors binding to specific genomic sites, such as LTR sequences.

name: Direct reprogramming
description: Direct reprogramming is a process of converting one cell type directly into another without passing through a pluripotent intermediate state.

name: Disease progression
description: Disease progression refers to the worsening or advancement of a disease over time.

name: Dispersion mechanism
description: The molecular and cellular processes by which transposable elements spread to new genomic locations.

name: Disruption of coding region
description: Disruption of a coding region occurs when a genetic element inserts into an exon, altering the protein-coding sequence.

name: Disruption of open reading frame (ORF)
description: Disruption of the open reading frame (ORF) refers to mutations that prevent the translation of a functional protein from a gene.

name: Distal enhancer activity
description: The function of a non-coding DNA sequence located far from the gene it regulates to increase the transcription of that gene.

name: Divergence-based insertion time estimation
description: A method to estimate the insertion time of an LTR retrotransposon by measuring the sequence divergence between its two LTRs, which were identical at the time of insertion.

name: Diversification
description: Diversification refers to the process by which genomic segments or sequences become more varied over time.

name: Diversification of transcriptional profiles
description: The process of generating variation in the patterns of gene expression across different conditions or cell types.

name: Domain shuffling
description: Domain shuffling is a mechanism of gene creation where new combinations of functional domains are assembled.

name: Domestication
description: Domestication refers to the evolutionary process by which transposable element-derived sequences are co-opted by the host genome to perform new cellular functions.

name: Domestication of TFBS
description: Domestication of TFBS refers to the hypothesized evolutionary process where transcription factor binding sites within ERVs/LRs are co-opted and reshaped by the host genome for regulatory functions.

name: Donor-specific assembly
description: Donor-specific assembly is a method to construct genome sequences specific to an individual or sample.

name: Double template switch
description: A specific type of RNA template switching event where the reverse transcriptase jumps between two different RNA templates, resulting in a tripartite chimeric cDNA.

name: Double-strand DNA break induction
description: Double-strand DNA break induction refers to the creation of breaks in both strands of the DNA duplex, which can be caused by the L1 endonuclease during target site cleavage.

name: Double-strand DNA breaks
description: Double-strand DNA breaks are a type of severe DNA damage where both strands of the DNA helix are severed.

name: Double-strand break (DSB) generation
description: Double-strand break generation refers to the creation of DNA double-strand breaks, which can be induced by the endonuclease activity of LINE-1 ORF2p during target-primed reverse transcription.

name: Double-strand break (DSB) repair
description: Double-strand break repair is a cellular mechanism to fix DNA double-strand breaks, crucial for genomic stability.

name: Double-strand break formation
description: The generation of breaks in both strands of the DNA double helix.

name: Double-strand break initiation
description: Double-strand break initiation is an event that can lead to homologous recombination.

name: Double-stranded DNA break (DSB)
description: Double-stranded DNA breaks (DSBs) are a severe form of genotoxic stress that threaten genome integrity and can activate cell cycle checkpoints or cause cell death.

name: Down-regulation of LINE-1 promoter activity
description: TRIM5α-induced signaling activates transcription factors AP-1 and NF-κB, leading to the down-regulation of LINE-1 promoter activity.

name: Downregulation of pro-survival signaling
description: A decrease in the activity of signaling pathways that promote cell survival, such as those involving AKT, mTORC1, and AMPK.

name: Drive evolution
description: The ability of retrotransposons to contribute to genomic changes and species-specific adaptations over time.

name: Drug resistance
description: Drug resistance is the reduction in effectiveness of a medication in treating a disease.

name: Drug selection-based enrichment
description: Drug selection-based enrichment is a method to isolate cells that have undergone a desired genetic modification by applying selective pressure.

name: Dual priming
description: A postulated mechanism that could generate truncated L1s with a poly(T) simple repeat at the 5' end of the insertion.

name: Duplication
description: Duplication refers to the process of copying genomic segments, which can include transposons.

name: Duplicative transposition
description: Duplicative transposition is a process that can lead to genomic instability and potential genome destruction.

name: Dynamic regulation
description: Dynamic regulation refers to the temporal control of gene or element activity, such as transient activation followed by suppression.

name: Dysregulation
description: The disruption of normal regulation, leading to abnormal expression levels or activity of genetic elements.

name: Dysregulation of DNA methylation
description: Dysregulation of DNA methylation refers to abnormal patterns of DNA methylation, including hypomethylation or hypermethylation, which can affect gene expression and genomic stability.

name: EMT programming
description: Epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition (EMT) programming is a process involved in cancer progression.

name: EN-independent retrotransposition (ENi)
description: A mechanism of L1 retrotransposition that does not require the L1 endonuclease activity, instead utilizing pre-existing 3'-OH groups at genomic DNA lesions, dysfunctional telomeres, or stalled replication forks to prime reverse transcription.

name: EN非依赖性逆转录转座
description: LINE-1转座子不依赖其自身核酸内切酶切割，而是利用基因组DNA损伤、功能失调的端粒或停滞的复制叉等位点产生的3'-羟基末端来启动逆转录的转座机制。

name: ERK激活
description: 细胞外信号调节激酶信号通路的活化过程。

name: ERV-specific repression
description: ERV-specific repression is a targeted mechanism that inhibits the expression and activity of specific endogenous retrovirus subfamilies.

name: ESCRT complex interaction
description: ESCRT (endosomal sorting complex required for transport) complex interaction refers to the conserved interaction between LINE-1 and the host ESCRT membrane budding machinery, which facilitates L1 replication.

name: ESCRT相互作用
description: LINE-1通过其晚期结构域基序与ESCRT（运输所需的内体分选复合体）膜出芽复合体发生相互作用。

name: Early DNA replication
description: The process by which specific genomic regions are replicated early during the S phase of the cell cycle.

name: Early embryonic control of L1
description: The evolutionary dynamic process by which newly emerged L1 lineages are suppressed in early embryogenesis.

name: Early termination
description: Early termination refers to the premature cessation of protein translation, often resulting in a truncated, non-functional protein.

name: Ectopic L1 incorporation
description: Ectopic L1 incorporation refers to the insertion of LINE-1 elements into the genome at new locations.

name: Ectopic excision
description: A proposed mechanism for the nonrandom genomic distribution of transposons involving imprecise removal and relocation.

name: Ectopic expression
description: Ectopic expression refers to the expression of a gene or element in an abnormal location, cell type, or at an abnormal time.

name: Ectopic gene expression
description: The abnormal expression of a gene in a tissue, cell type, or developmental stage where it is not normally expressed.

name: Efficient delivery of non-viral vectors
description: Efficient delivery of non-viral vectors involves methods to introduce genetic material (e.g., transposons, mRNA, plasmids) into target cells without using viral particles.

name: Elevated retrotransposon activity
