description: T cell engineering is the process of modifying T cells, often to express chimeric antigen receptors (CARs), for therapeutic purposes.

name: T cell migration
description: T cell migration is the directed movement of T cells towards sites of inflammation or infection.

name: T-cell activity
description: T-cell activity refers to the functions of T lymphocytes, which are central to adaptive immunity.

name: TAD boundary formation
description: TAD (topologically associating domain) boundary formation refers to the establishment of genomic regions that restrict interactions between adjacent chromatin domains.

name: TAD hierarchy disruption
description: TAD hierarchy disruption refers to the perturbation of the hierarchical organization of topologically associating domains (TADs) in the 3D genome architecture.

name: TE activation
description: TE activation refers to the increased expression or mobilization of transposable elements.

name: TE amplification
description: The process by which transposable elements increase their copy number within a genome.

name: TE dysregulation
description: TE dysregulation refers to the abnormal expression or activity of transposable elements.

name: TE exaptation
description: TE exaptation refers to the process by which transposable elements are co-opted by the host genome for new functions.

name: TE expression
description: TE expression refers to the transcription of transposable elements into RNA.

name: TE expression QTL (teQTL)
description: TE expression QTL (teQTL) refers to genetic loci that influence the expression levels of transposable elements.

name: TE genotyping
description: TE genotyping is the process of determining the allelic status of transposable element insertions in an individual's genome.

name: TE insertion
description: TE insertion refers to the integration of a transposable element into a new genomic location.

name: TE insertion mutation
description: TE insertion mutation refers to a mutation caused by the insertion of a transposable element into a genomic locus.

name: TE insertional activity
description: TE insertional activity refers to the somatic insertion of transposable elements into new genomic locations.

name: TE insertions
description: TE insertions refer to the integration of transposable element sequences into new genomic locations.

name: TE mobilization
description: Transposable element mobilization is the process by which TEs move or copy themselves to new genomic locations.

name: TE nesting
description: TE nesting refers to the insertion of transposable elements (TEs) into existing TEs in the genome.

name: TE proliferation
description: TE proliferation refers to the replication and increase in copy number of transposable elements within a genome.

name: TE repression
description: TE repression is the process of silencing transposable elements to prevent their activity and potential genomic damage.

name: TE silencing
description: TE silencing refers to the molecular mechanisms that repress the expression and activity of transposable elements.

name: TE transcriptional activation
description: Transposable element transcriptional activation refers to the increased expression of transposable elements.

name: TE transcriptional network co-expression regulation
description: The coordinated regulation of transposable element expression networks, often involving transcription factors.

name: TE variant detection
description: TE variant detection is the process of identifying the presence or absence of transposable element sequences in genomic data.

name: TE-derived promoter activity
description: TE-derived promoter activity refers to the ability of transposable element sequences to initiate transcription of downstream genes.

name: TE-mediated damage
description: TE-mediated damage refers to genomic damage caused by the activity of transposable elements.

name: TE-mediated deletion
description: TE-mediated deletion is a structural variation event where the presence or activity of a transposable element leads to the removal of a genomic segment.

name: TE沉默
description: PIWI通路通过非经典piRNA靶向转座子，抑制其活性。

name: TF binding site contribution
description: The process by which transposable elements provide functional binding sites for transcription factors.

name: TGF-β1 signaling pathway
description: TGF-β1 signaling pathway is a canonical signaling pathway involved in various cellular processes.

name: TGF-β1/Smad pathway
description: The TGF-β1/Smad pathway is a signaling cascade that can be involved in regulating gene expression and cellular processes.

name: TGF-β1信号通路激活
description: 本文发现的介导AhR配体激活LINE-1的经典信号通路。

name: TGFβ-signalling activation
description: TGFβ-signalling activation is a key oncogenic pathway involved in cell growth, differentiation, and apoptosis, often dysregulated in cancer.

name: TGFβ信号通路激活
description: 转化生长因子β信号通路的活化状态。

name: THE1B fusion transcript expression
description: THE1B fusion transcript expression refers to the transcription of chimeric RNA molecules containing THE1B sequences.

name: TIR-DNA-binding
description: TIR-DNA-binding is the activity of the Hsmar1 transposase domain within SETMAR to specifically recognize and bind to terminal inverted repeat (TIR) sequences.

name: TIR-specific DNA-binding activity
description: TIR-specific DNA-binding activity is the ability to bind specifically to Terminal Inverted Repeat sequences of transposons.

name: TNF-induced gene expression
description: TNF-induced gene expression is the activation of specific genes in response to stimulation by the proinflammatory cytokine Tumor Necrosis Factor (TNF).

name: TP53-dependent growth arrest
description: TP53依赖性生长停滞是细胞在DNA损伤等应激条件下，由p53蛋白介导的细胞周期停滞。

name: TP53-mediated TE regulation
description: TP53-mediated TE regulation refers to the process by which the TP53 tumor suppressor protein directly or indirectly controls the expression of transposable elements.

name: TRIM41-mediated ORF2p ubiquitination and degradation
description: This is a mechanism where the E3 ligase TRIM41 ubiquitinates the L1-encoded ORF2p protein, targeting it for proteasomal degradation.

name: TRIM5α-induced innate immune signaling
description: TRIM5α acts as a pattern recognition receptor and induces innate immune signaling upon interaction with LINE-1 ribonucleoprotein complexes.

name: TRIM5α-mediated restriction of LINE-1
description: The intrinsic immune factor TRIM5α senses and restricts LINE-1 retroelements by interacting with LINE-1 ribonucleoprotein complexes in the cytoplasm.

name: TTAA target-site specific insertion
description: TTAA target-site specific insertion is a characteristic insertion mechanism of the piggyBac transposon.

name: Tailless retropseudogene formation
description: The formation of retropseudogenes that lack the characteristic oligo(A) tail, often resulting from LINE-1-mediated retrotransposition.

name: Target site cleavage
description: The process of cleaving the genomic DNA at the target site for L1 insertion.

name: Target site duplication
description: Target site duplication is a short, direct repeat of genomic DNA flanking a newly inserted transposable element, generated during the integration process.

name: Target site duplication (TSD)
description: A hallmark feature of L1 retrotransposition, where short direct repeats flank the insertion site.

name: Target site recognition and cleavage
description: The process by which L1-Endonuclease specifically recognizes and cleaves the TA junction DNA sequence (5'-TTTT/AA-3') to initiate integration during retrotransposition.

name: Target site selection
description: Target site selection refers to the process by which a transposon's endonuclease domain recognizes and cleaves specific DNA sequences for insertion.

name: Target site-primed reverse transcription (TPRT)
description: Target site-primed reverse transcription is the mechanism by which LINE-1 copies its transcripts and those of other genetic elements into genomic DNA.

name: Target-primed retrotransposition
description: Target-primed retrotransposition is the replication mechanism of non-LTR retrotransposons, such as LINE-1, involving reverse transcription at the site of insertion.

name: Target-primed reverse transcription (TPRT)
description: Target-primed reverse transcription is the mechanism by which LINE-1 elements initiate reverse transcription from a nick in target DNA.

name: Target-site duplication (TSD) generation
description: The creation of short direct repeats flanking a newly inserted transposon, resulting from staggered cuts at the target site.

name: Target-site preference
description: Mammalian L1s show target preference for the sequence 5'-TTAAAA-3'.

name: Target-site primed reverse transcription
description: The process used by L1 elements to mobilize, which involves both the L1 endonuclease and reverse transcriptase.

name: Targeted DNA cleavage
description: Targeted DNA cleavage is a coordinated reaction involved in the retrotransposition pathway of NLRs.

name: Targeted DNA integration
description: The process of integrating DNA into a specific genomic locus, which is needed for improved safety in gene delivery applications.

name: Targeted DNA methylation
description: Targeted DNA methylation is an epigenetic modification technique that specifically adds methyl groups to cytosine bases in defined genomic regions.

name: Targeted gene addition
description: Targeted gene addition is a method for inserting therapeutic genes into specific genomic locations.

name: Targeted insertion
description: Targeted insertion refers to the process of directing a genetic element to integrate at a specific genomic sequence.

name: Targeted integration
description: The directed insertion of DNA, such as retrotransposons, into specific genomic locations, influenced by factors beyond just endonuclease specificity.

name: Targeted mRNA decay via short imperfect base-pairing
description: A post-transcriptional regulatory mechanism where RNA molecules with imperfect complementarity bind to target mRNAs, leading to their degradation.

name: Targeted transgene integration
description: Targeted transgene integration is a genome engineering technique for inserting specific DNA sequences into precise genomic locations.

name: Targeted transposition
description: Targeted transposition is the process of directing transposon integration to specific, user-defined genomic locations.

name: Targeting
description: Targeting refers to the directed localization of a protein or complex to specific genomic sites.

name: Targeting gene-poor regions
description: A strong preference for LINE-1 retrotransposition to insert into gene-poor regions of the genome, such as those on chromosome 13.

name: Targeting of Alu repeats by microRNAs
description: A process where microRNAs bind to complementary sequences within Alu repeats integrated in mRNAs, potentially regulating gene expression.

name: Targeting of retrotransposon integration
description: The process by which the integration of retrotransposons is directed to specific genomic regions.

name: Telomere dysfunction
description: Telomere dysfunction refers to the loss of protective function at chromosome ends, leading to genomic instability.

name: Telomere homeostasis
description: Telomere homeostasis refers to the maintenance of proper telomere length and stability.

name: Template-jumping
description: Template-jumping is a proposed mechanism by which the L1-encoded reverse transcriptase switches templates during reverse transcription, potentially leading to extra nucleotide additions.

name: Templated Sequence Insertion Polymorphism (TSIP)
description: TSIP is a form of polymorphism where a sequence templated from a distant genomic region is inserted into the genome.

name: Tet1-mediated activation of L1
description: Tet1蛋白介导的L1激活，指Tet1蛋白通过修饰L1启动子区域的DNA甲基化状态来激活L1转座子的过程。

name: Tethering mechanism
description: Tethering mechanism is a process that directs integrating genetic elements into specific genomic regions, such as transcriptional start sites.

name: Tetraploidy
description: Tetraploidy refers to the state of having four copies of a genomic segment or chromosome.

name: Three-dimensional genome changes
description: Alterations in the spatial organization and folding of chromatin within the nucleus.

name: Tissue adaptation
description: Tissue adaptation is the process by which immune cells acquire specialized functions to reside and operate within specific organs.

name: Tissue-shared regulatory region formation
description: The process by which specific genomic regions acquire regulatory activity (e.g., promoter or enhancer function) that is conserved across multiple tissues.

name: Tissue-specific gene regulation
description: The process by which gene expression is controlled in a manner specific to particular tissues or cell types.

name: Tissue-specific promoter activity
description: Tissue-specific promoter activity refers to the initiation of gene transcription primarily in specific tissues or cell types.

name: Tissue-specific regulation
description: Tissue-specific regulation refers to the modulation of gene expression that occurs in a particular tissue type.

name: Tissue-specific regulatory region formation
description: The process by which specific genomic regions acquire regulatory activity (e.g., promoter or enhancer function) in a tissue-specific manner.

name: Tissue-specific splicing
description: Tissue-specific splicing refers to exons whose inclusion in the mature mRNA transcript varies depending on the tissue type.

name: Tissue-specific transcription
description: The activity of L1 ASP is tissue-specific, potentially expanding the expression pattern of adjacent host genes.

name: Toll样受体4信号通路
description: 一种模式识别受体信号通路，在本研究中被证明介导了吗啡和枸橼酸芬太尼诱导的LINE-1逆转录转座。

name: Trans-mobilization
description: Trans-mobilization is the process where autonomous retrotransposons (like LINE-1) mobilize non-autonomous elements (like SINEs and processed pseudogenes).

name: Trans-regulation
description: Trans-regulation refers to the regulation of gene expression by a genetic element located on a different chromosome.

name: Transcript co-option
description: L1-derived transcripts are co-opted as chimeric transcripts or regulatory RNAs.

name: Transcript generation
description: L1s generate hundreds of developmentally regulated and cell type-specific transcripts.

name: Transcript half-life regulation
description: Control of the stability or degradation rate of RNA transcripts, affecting their persistence in the cell.

name: Transcript incorporation
description: The process by which transposable element-derived sequences become part of coding or noncoding RNA transcripts.

name: Transcript isoform usage
description: Transcript isoform usage refers to the relative abundance or selection of different mRNA splice variants produced from a single gene.

name: Transcript splitting
description: The process where a single gene transcript is divided into two separate transcripts due to the insertion of an L1 element.

name: Transcript steady-state level regulation
description: Control of the abundance of RNA transcripts within the cell under equilibrium conditions.

name: Transcript structure modulation
description: Alteration of the architecture or composition of RNA transcripts, which can affect their stability, localization, or function.

name: Transcription
description: The process of synthesizing RNA from a DNA template.

name: Transcription by RNA polymerase III
description: SINEs are transcribed by RNA polymerase III, generating noncoding RNA.

name: Transcription disruption
description: Transcription disruption refers to the interference with normal gene transcription processes.

name: Transcription dysregulation
description: Transcription dysregulation refers to the abnormal control of gene transcription, which can be caused by various mechanisms including retrotransposon insertions.

name: Transcription elongation
description: Transcription elongation is the process of synthesizing RNA from a DNA template, which can be defective in native L1 elements due to high adenosine content.

name: Transcription factor binding site (TFBS) derivation
description: The process by which repetitive DNA sequences, including transposable elements, serve as origins for functional transcription factor binding sites.

name: Transcription from core promoter
description: The initiation of RNA synthesis from a gene's core promoter region.

name: Transcription from repetitive sequences
description: The process of generating RNA transcripts from repetitive DNA sequences in the genome.

name: Transcription initiation
description: Transcription initiation is the process of beginning RNA synthesis from a DNA template.

name: Transcription of intergenic RNAs
description: The process of synthesizing RNA molecules from intergenic regions of the genome.

name: Transcription regulation
description: Transcription regulation refers to the control of gene or element expression at the transcriptional level.

name: Transcription termination
description: Transcription termination is the process of ending RNA synthesis at specific genomic sites.

name: Transcription-coupled DNA repair (TCR)
description: Transcription-coupled DNA repair (TCR) is a DNA repair pathway that preferentially removes lesions from the transcribed strand of active genes.

name: Transcription-coupled nucleotide excision repair
description: A sub-pathway of nucleotide excision repair that preferentially repairs lesions in the transcribed strand of active genes.

name: Transcriptional activity
description: Transcriptional activity refers to the process of transcribing DNA into RNA, indicating gene or element expression.

name: Transcriptional activity of transposable elements
description: The process of transcribing transposable element sequences into RNA, which is often linked to cancer development when epigenetic repression is lifted.

name: Transcriptional alteration
description: Transcriptional alteration refers to changes in gene expression levels or patterns, including changes in splicing.

name: Transcriptional control by external promoters
description: Transcriptional control by external promoters is a mechanism where SVA source elements are regulated by a multitude of cellular promoters in germ cells.

name: Transcriptional control via LINE-1 ASP
description: The regulation of gene expression by the LINE-1 antisense promoter (ASP).

name: Transcriptional de-repression
description: Transcriptional de-repression refers to the release of transcriptional silencing, often associated with epigenetic changes like DNA demethylation.

name: Transcriptional defect
description: Transcriptional defect refers to an abnormality in the process of gene transcription, leading to reduced or aberrant mRNA production.

name: Transcriptional defense
description: Transcriptional defense mechanisms suppress transposable element activity at the level of transcription.

name: Transcriptional downregulation
description: A decrease in the level of transcription of specific genetic elements.

name: Transcriptional dysregulation
description: Transcriptional dysregulation refers to the abnormal or disrupted control of gene expression at the transcriptional level.

name: Transcriptional elongation defect
description: A transcriptional elongation defect in LINE-1 open reading frames inhibits the robust expression of L1 RNA and proteins.

name: Transcriptional enhancement
description: Transcriptional enhancement is the process of increasing or promoting gene transcription.

name: Transcriptional enhancer activity
description: The ability of a DNA sequence to increase the transcription of cis-linked genes.

name: Transcriptional flexibility
description: L1 ASP-driven transcription may increase the transcriptional flexibility of several human genes.

name: Transcriptional gene silencing
description: Transcriptional gene silencing is an epigenetic mechanism that represses gene expression at the transcriptional level.

name: Transcriptional inactivation
description: Transcriptional inactivation refers to the silencing of gene or transposon expression.

name: Transcriptional induction
description: Transcriptional induction is the process of increasing the rate of transcription of a specific genetic element.

name: Transcriptional inhibition in cis
description: Transcriptional inhibition in cis refers to the silencing of gene expression by a regulatory element located on the same DNA molecule.

name: Transcriptional program activation
description: Transcriptional program activation refers to the induction of a specific set of genes by a transcription factor.

name: Transcriptional reactivation
description: Transcriptional reactivation refers to the process where previously silenced genes or elements become transcriptionally active again.

name: Transcriptional regulation
description: Transcriptional regulation is the control of gene expression at the level of transcription.

name: Transcriptional regulation of trophoblast syncytialization
description: The process of controlling gene expression during the fusion of trophoblast cells to form multinucleated syncytiotrophoblasts.

name: Transcriptional regulation via DLX3
description: The mechanism by which the transcription factor distal-less homeobox 3 (DLX3) interacts with a genomic site to regulate gene expression.

name: Transcriptional regulator binding site formation
description: The process by which repeat sequences become functional elements, such as binding sites for transcriptional regulators.

name: Transcriptional repression
description: Transcriptional repression is a process that decreases the rate of gene transcription, often involving histone modifications and heterochromatin formation.

name: Transcriptional reprogramming
description: A global change in gene expression profiles.

name: Transcriptional suppression
description: Transcriptional suppression refers to the inhibition of gene or transposon expression at the transcriptional level.

name: Transcriptome alteration
description: Transcriptome alteration refers to changes in the expression pattern of genes, which can be caused by nearby transposon insertions.

name: Transcriptome complexity
description: L1-derived transcripts provide a layer of primate- and human-specific transcriptome complexity.

name: Transcriptome influence
description: Transcriptome influence refers to the effect of a genetic variant, such as a transposon insertion, on the complete set of RNA transcripts in a cell.

name: Transcriptomic changes
description: Changes in the complete set of RNA transcripts produced by the genome in response to stimuli.

name: Transcriptomic regulation
description: The regulation of gene expression programs, which can be affected by transposon activity.

name: Transfection
description: Transfection is the process of deliberately introducing nucleic acids into eukaryotic cells, often using vectors or physical methods.

name: Transfer of non-L1 DNA
description: A mechanism where LINE-1 retrotransposition transfers flanking non-L1 DNA sequences to new genomic locations.

name: Transgene excision
description: Transgene excision is the process of removing an introduced gene from the genome, often leaving no residual sequence (scarless).

name: Transgene expression
description: Transgene expression is the process by which a gene that has been introduced into an organism is transcribed and translated to produce a functional protein.

name: Transgene reactivation
description: The re-expression of residual reprogramming transgenes upon induction of cell differentiation.

name: Transgene removal
description: Transgene removal is the process of excising exogenous genetic material after it has served its purpose in reprogramming.

name: Transgenesis
description: Transgenesis is the introduction of foreign DNA into a genome, facilitated by transposon systems.

name: Transient hyperactivation
description: Transient hyperactivation is a temporary, high-level increase in the activity of a genetic element.

name: Translation
description: Translation is the cellular process of synthesizing proteins from messenger RNA (mRNA).

name: Translation enhancement
description: Translation enhancement is the process of increasing the efficiency of protein synthesis from an mRNA molecule.

name: Translation on free cytoplasmic ribosomes
description: Protein synthesis occurring on ribosomes not associated with the endoplasmic reticulum, typically for soluble proteins.

name: Translation regulation
description: Translation regulation controls the efficiency of protein synthesis from mRNA.

name: Translation reinitiation
description: Translation reinitiation is a process where a ribosome that has terminated translation at one stop codon can initiate translation at a downstream start codon.

name: Translational regulation
description: Translational regulation refers to the control of protein synthesis at the level of mRNA translation.

name: Translocation
description: Translocation refers to the movement of a genomic segment from one location to another, which can involve transposons.

name: Transposase activity
description: Transposase activity refers to the enzymatic function that catalyzes the movement of transposable elements within the genome.

name: Transposase functionality
description: Transposase functionality refers to the enzymatic activity associated with transposable elements that can mediate their movement and potentially influence genomic stability.

name: Transposase gene splicing
description: Transposase gene splicing is the process where the transposase gene of a transposon is spliced to a host cellular gene, potentially affecting its expression or function.

name: Transposase overproduction inhibition
description: Transposase overproduction inhibition is a mechanism where high concentrations of transposase protein inhibit further transposition activity.

name: Transposase rodlet formation
description: Transposase rodlet formation is the process by which transposase proteins assemble into filamentous structures, which is associated with the cessation of transposition activity.

name: Transposition activity
description: Transposition activity refers to the ability of transposable elements to move and replicate within genomes over evolutionary time.

name: Transposition burst
description: A transposition burst refers to a period of high activity and increased copy number of transposable elements in a genome.

name: Transposition into and fragmentation of other TEs
description: The process where a transposable element inserts into and fragments another, older transposable element already present in the genome.

name: Transposition repression
description: Transposition repression refers to the mechanisms that inhibit the movement or 'jumping' of transposable elements within the genome.

name: Transposition restriction
description: Transposition restriction refers to the cellular mechanisms that suppress or inhibit the uncontrolled movement of transposable elements like LINE-1.

name: Transposon de-silencing
description: Transposon de-silencing refers to the loss of repression, leading to increased transposon expression and potential activity.

name: Transposon dispersal
description: Transposon dispersal is the process by which transposable elements spread to new genomic locations.

name: Transposon expression
description: Transposon expression refers to the transcription and potential translation of transposable element sequences.

name: Transposon insertion
description: Transposon insertion is the process by which transposons relocate and integrate into new genomic locations.

name: Transposon insertion polymorphism
description: Transposon insertion polymorphism refers to genetic variation in a population caused by the presence or absence of a transposon insertion at a specific genomic locus.

name: Transposon integration
description: Transposon integration refers to the insertion of a transposon into the host genome.

name: Transposon mutagenesis
description: Transposon mutagenesis is a technique that uses transposable elements to create insertional mutations in the genome.

name: Transposon proliferation
description: Transposon proliferation is an evolutionary mechanism that increases genome complexity.

name: Transposon regulation
description: Transposon regulation encompasses the cellular mechanisms that control the activity and expression of transposable elements.

name: Transposon silencing
description: Transposon silencing is the process of repressing transposon activity and expression to maintain genomic stability.

name: Transposon suppression system
description: A proposed system to explain why transposon-derived repeats seldom cause mutations in humans.

name: Transposon-mediated gene delivery
description: Transposon-mediated gene delivery is a technique that utilizes transposon systems to stably integrate genetic material into a host cell's genome.

name: Transposon-mediated gene transfer
description: Transposon-mediated gene transfer is a method for introducing genetic material into cells using transposon systems as vectors.

name: Transposon-mediated indels
description: Insertions and deletions in the genome that are mediated by the activity of transposable elements.

name: Trimerization
description: Trimerization is the process of forming a complex consisting of three identical or similar subunits.

name: Tumor driver
description: Tumor driver refers to a mechanism or factor, such as aberrant expression of satellite RNA, that promotes the initiation or progression of cancer.

name: Tumor invasion
description: Tumor invasion is the process by which cancer cells spread into surrounding tissues.

name: Tumor suppression
description: A biological process that inhibits the formation or progression of tumors.

name: Tumor-exclusive expression
description: Expression of L1 retrotransposons that is detected only in tumor cells and is absent in normal cells and tissues.

name: Tumor-specific antigen generation
description: Tumor-specific antigen generation is the process of creating peptides that are uniquely or predominantly presented by tumor cells, which can be recognized by the immune system.

name: Twin priming
description: Twin priming is a proposed mechanism for the creation of inversions in L1 retrotransposition.

name: Twin-priming mechanism
description: Twin-priming is a mechanism that can lead to rearrangements during L1 retrotransposition.

name: Type I interferon response
description: An innate immune signaling pathway activated by cytosolic nucleic acids, such as those from retrotransposons.

name: T细胞活性
description: T淋巴细胞识别抗原、增殖并执行免疫效应的功能。

name: U6 snRNA retrotransposition
description: The retrotransposition of U6 small nuclear RNA, which requires both LINE-1 ORF1p and ORF2p.

name: U6 snRNA转座
description: L1逆转录转座机制能够将U6小核RNA动员到新的基因组位置。

name: U6 snRNA逆转录转座
description: U6小核RNA被LINE-1编码的蛋白质动员，整合到基因组中的过程。

name: U6/L1 chimeric RNA formation
description: The process of forming chimeric RNAs consisting of a copy of U6 small nuclear RNA (snRNA) conjoined to a variably 5'-truncated L1 RNA.

name: U6/L1 pseudogene formation
description: The generation of processed pseudogenes resulting from the retrotransposition of U6/L1 chimeric RNAs.

name: U6/L1假基因形成
description: U6/L1嵌合RNA发生逆转录转座，导致基因组中产生U6/L1假基因。

name: U6/L1嵌合RNA形成
description: 由U6小核RNA与5‘端截短的L1 RNA连接形成嵌合RNA的过程。

name: Ubiquitin-proteasome system
description: The ubiquitin-proteasome system is a major pathway for the degradation of intracellular proteins.

name: Underrepresentation in epigenetic studies
description: The systematic omission of ambiguous sequencing reads leads to an incomplete and biased assessment of the epigenetic state of recently active transposable elements.

name: Unequal recombination
description: Unequal recombination is a type of genetic recombination that can occur between misaligned repetitive sequences, such as transposons, leading to genomic rearrangements.

name: Unequal sister chromatid exchange
description: Unequal sister chromatid exchange is a recombination event between non-allelic sequences on sister chromatids, leading to duplications and deletions.

name: Upregulation
description: Upregulation refers to an increase in the expression or activity of a biological element.

name: Uridylation
description: Uridylation is a post-transcriptional RNA modification involving the addition of uridine residues to the 3' end of RNA molecules.

name: Utilization of DNA repair machinery
description: Retrotransposons can co-opt cellular DNA repair enzymes to complete their replication cycle.

name: VNTR activity repression
description: The inhibition of the activity of variable number tandem repeats (VNTRs) in a gene's 3' UTR.

name: VNTR expansion/contraction
description: VNTR expansion/contraction is a microhomology-driven mechanism that mediates changes in the length of the VNTR domain at the DNA level.

name: VNTR结构域决定动员效率
description: 在LAVA转座子中，其中心的可变数目串联重复序列结构域是决定其被LINE-1反式动员效率的关键因素。

name: Variable number of tandem repeat (VNTR) region
description: A region within a transposon characterized by tandemly repeated sequences that vary in copy number.

name: Variable transcription initiation
description: The phenomenon where transcription initiation sites for L1 are not restricted to a single nucleotide but vary considerably in both downstream and upstream directions.

name: Vasculogenesis
description: Vasculogenesis is the de novo formation of blood vessels from endothelial progenitor cells.

name: Vector removal
description: Vector removal is the process of excising or eliminating the delivery vehicle (e.g., transposon vector) from the host genome after it has served its purpose.

name: Ventricular enlargement
description: An increase in the size of the heart's ventricles.

name: Viral DNA integration
description: Viral DNA integration is the process by which viral genetic material inserts into the host genome.

name: Viral RNA integration
description: Viral RNA integration refers to the process by which viral RNA sequences are reverse-transcribed and inserted into the host genome.

name: Viral genome integration
description: Viral genome integration refers to the insertion of viral DNA into the host genome.

name: Viral particle production
description: Viral particle production is the assembly and release of complete viral particles.

name: Viral protein production
description: Viral protein production is the synthesis of proteins encoded by viral elements.

name: Viral sequence integration
description: Viral sequence integration refers to the incorporation of viral genetic material into the host genome.

name: Viral-host chimeric transcription
description: Viral-host chimeric transcription refers to the expression of transcripts containing sequences from both integrated viral DNA and the host genome.

name: Virus-host genomic integration
description: Virus-host genomic integration refers to the process where viral DNA is inserted into the host genome.

name: X chromosome inactivation
description: X chromosome inactivation is the process by which one of the two X chromosomes in female mammals is transcriptionally silenced to achieve dosage compensation.

name: X inactivation
description: X inactivation is the process by which one of the two X chromosomes in female mammals is transcriptionally silenced to achieve dosage compensation.

name: YY1-driven transcription from LTR promoter
description: YY1 transcription factor binds to the cryptic MER52A LTR promoter and drives transcription of lncMER52A in hepatocellular carcinoma.

name: YY1-mediated transcription regulation
description: Transcription regulation involving the nuclear factor YY1, which can function in both RNA polymerase II and III systems.

name: YY1结合位点介导的DNA甲基化
description: 保守的YY1转录因子结合位点介导了L1启动子在多能细胞和分化细胞中的DNA甲基化。

name: ZAP-mediated inhibition of retrotransposition
description: Zinc-finger antiviral protein (ZAP) inhibits the retrotransposition of LINE and Alu elements by reducing the accumulation of full-length L1 RNA and L1-encoded proteins.

name: ZAP与L1核糖核蛋白颗粒结合
description: ZAP蛋白与L1的核糖核蛋白颗粒发生相互作用，这通过免疫共沉淀和共定位实验证实。

name: ZAP抗逆转录元件活性
description: ZAP蛋白通过多种机制（如RNA降解、伴侣蛋白复合物、染色质修饰等）在细胞质和细胞核中抑制逆转录元件的活性。

name: aberrant DNA methylation
description: Aberrant DNA methylation refers to abnormal patterns of DNA methylation, such as the hypomethylation of retrotransposons, which occurs during malignant cell transformation.

name: aberrant THE1B fusion transcript expression
description: Aberrant THE1B fusion transcript expression refers to the elevated or dysregulated expression of THE1B-containing fusion transcripts in disease states.

name: aberrant activation
description: Aberrant activation refers to the abnormal or dysregulated expression or activity of a gene or element, such as HERVs.

name: aberrant activity
description: Aberrant activity refers to abnormal or dysregulated biological function, such as uncontrolled transposition.

name: aberrant expression
description: 异常表达，指基因或转座子的表达水平或模式偏离正常状态。

name: aberrant gene regulation
description: Aberrant gene regulation refers to the disruption of normal gene expression patterns.

name: aberrant immune responses
description: Aberrant immune responses are inappropriate or chronic immune system reactions.

name: aberrant oncogene activation
description: Aberrant oncogene activation is the inappropriate or dysregulated transcriptional activation of genes that can promote cancer development.

name: aberrant reactivation
description: Aberrant reactivation refers to the inappropriate re-expression of normally silenced genetic elements.

name: aberrant regulation
description: Aberrant regulation of transposable elements can result in deleterious effects.

name: aberrant splice-site activation
description: Aberrant splice-site activation is the process where a cryptic or non-canonical splice site is used, often due to mutations, leading to altered mRNA splicing.

name: abnormal DNA rearrangement
description: Abnormal DNA rearrangement refers to genomic alterations such as deletions, insertions, or translocations that deviate from the normal structure.

name: abnormal gene expression
description: Abnormal gene expression refers to the dysregulation of gene transcription levels.

name: abnormal programming
description: Abnormal programming refers to the incorrect establishment or alteration of epigenetic or other regulatory patterns during development.

name: abscopal effect
description: The abscopal effect is a phenomenon where localized radiotherapy induces systemic anti-tumor effects at distant, non-irradiated sites.

name: accumulation on the X chromosome
description: The process by which transposable elements, such as LINE-1, become enriched on the X chromosome.

name: activating oncogenic transcriptional factors
description: Activating oncogenic transcriptional factors is a process that increases the activity of transcription factors that promote cancer development.

name: activation mutagenesis screen
description: Activation mutagenesis screen is a forward genetic approach using transposons to randomly activate gene expression and identify genes conferring specific phenotypes, such as drug resistance.

name: activation of L1s
description: Activation of L1s refers to the process by which LINE-1 retrotransposons become transcriptionally and transpositionally active, often due to epigenetic changes like hypomethylation.

name: activation of MDA5
description: The process by which the innate immune receptor MDA5 is activated, typically by binding to double-stranded RNA.

name: activation of cGAS-STING pathway
description: The process of triggering the cyclic GMP-AMP synthase (cGAS)-stimulator of interferon genes (STING) innate immune signaling pathway.

name: activation of dormant repeat elements
description: Activation of dormant repeat elements refers to the undesirable reactivation of transposable elements due to loss of epigenetic silencing.

name: activation of pluripotency genes
description: Activation of pluripotency genes refers to the process of turning on genes that are essential for maintaining the pluripotent state of stem cells.

name: activation of the innate immune system
description: Activation of the innate immune system is a cellular response that can be triggered by transposable element activity.

name: activation of tumor-suppressive mechanisms
description: The initiation of cellular processes, such as p53 and interferon responses, that inhibit tumor development.

name: active retrotransposition
description: Active retrotransposition refers to the ongoing process of LINE-1 elements copying and inserting themselves into new genomic locations.

name: activities of L1 si/piRNAs, mRNAs, ORF1 and ORF2 proteins
description: L1 si/piRNAs、mRNAs、ORF1和ORF2蛋白的活性，这些分子活动参与疾病的发生发展。

name: adenosinergic immunometabolic suppression
description: Adenosinergic immunometabolic suppression refers to the immunosuppressive effects mediated by adenosine in the tumor microenvironment.

name: adjacent genes expression
description: LINE-1插入位点附近基因的表达水平，受LINE-1活动的影响。

name: adoptive NK cell immunotherapy
description: Adoptive NK cell immunotherapy involves the transfer of engineered or activated natural killer cells to a patient to treat diseases such as cancer.

name: affect gene transcription
description: Affect gene transcription refers to the process by which transposable elements can influence the expression of genes.

name: affect somatic genome structure and function
description: The process by which LINE-1 activity influences the architecture and operation of somatic cell genomes.

name: affecting gene expression or regulation
description: This refers to the process by which the insertion of a transposon can alter the normal expression patterns or regulatory mechanisms of nearby genes.

name: aging-associated myeloid-biased hematopoiesis
description: A shift in blood cell production towards the myeloid lineage that occurs with aging.

name: allele-specific methylation
description: 等位基因特异性甲基化，指转座子在不同等位基因上表现出不同的甲基化状态。

name: alter gene expression
description: Alteration of gene expression refers to changes in the level at which a gene is transcribed.

name: alteration of epigenetic regulations
description: Alteration of epigenetic regulations involves changes in heritable gene expression patterns not caused by changes in DNA sequence.

name: alteration of gene expression
description: Alteration of gene expression refers to changes in the level or pattern of gene transcription, which can be caused by transposon insertion into regulatory regions.

name: alteration of genomic landscape
description: Changes to the structure and organization of the genome, contributed by the mobilization of transposable elements.

name: alterations in target gene expression
description: Alterations in target gene expression refer to changes in the level or pattern of gene expression, potentially caused by retrotransposon insertion.

name: alterations to normal splicing patterns
description: A mechanism where SVA insertions disrupt the normal process of RNA splicing.

name: altered DNA methylation
description: Altered DNA methylation is a change in the methylation pattern of DNA.

name: altered chromatin landscapes
description: 改变的染色质景观是指基因组中染色质结构和修饰状态的变化。

name: altered expression
description: Altered expression refers to changes in the normal level of transcription or activity of transposable elements.

name: altered genetic networks
description: Altered genetic networks refer to changes in the complex interactions between genes and their products.

name: altered retrotransposon expression
description: 逆转录转座子表达改变，指转座子转录水平或活性发生异常变化。

name: altering gene activity
description: 逆转录转座子插入到基因中，改变该基因的活性或功能。

name: altering gene expression
description: The process by which retrotransposons modify the level or pattern of gene transcription.

name: alternate transcript activation
description: Alternate transcript activation refers to the initiation of transcription from an alternative promoter or start site, leading to a different RNA transcript.

name: alternative lengthening of telomeres (ALT)
description: Alternative lengthening of telomeres (ALT) is a telomere maintenance mechanism used by some cancer cells that do not express telomerase.

name: alternative mRNA transcript formation
description: Alternative mRNA transcript formation is the generation of different mRNA variants from a single gene.

name: alternative promoter activation
description: Alternative promoter activation refers to the initiation of transcription from a non-canonical promoter site, such as those within transposable elements.

name: alternative promoter usage
description: Alternative promoter usage is a mechanism where different promoters drive the expression of distinct isoforms of the same gene.

name: alternative promoters identification
description: The process of identifying different transcription start sites for a single gene, leading to variant transcripts.

name: alternative protein isoform production
description: The production of alternative protein isoforms from a single gene, often driven by different promoters, leading to proteins with distinct functions.

name: amino acid substitution
description: Amino acid substitution refers to changes in the protein sequence of a gene, which can indicate evolutionary selection pressure.

name: ancestral insertion
description: Ancestral insertion refers to a transposon insertion event that occurred in a common ancestor of a population and is now fixed or polymorphic in that population.

name: anchorage-independent growth
description: Anchorage-independent growth is the ability of cells to proliferate without being attached to a solid surface, a hallmark of cellular transformation.

name: aneuploidy generation
description: 非整倍体生成是指细胞染色体数目异常的过程。

name: angiogenesis
description: 血管生成，是从现有血管形成新血管的过程。

name: antisense RNA expression
description: Transcription of RNA from the strand opposite to the sense strand of a gene.

name: antisense promoter (ASP) activity
description: The antisense promoter activity is a function located in the L1Hs 5' untranslated region that drives transcription in the opposite direction.

name: antisense promoter (ASP)-driven transcription
description: The antisense promoter of LINE-1 drives transcription in the opposite direction into adjacent cellular sequences, yielding chimeric transcripts.

name: antisense promoter (L1-ASP) activity
description: The antisense promoter (L1-ASP) of LINE-1 elements drives the transcription of chimeric transcripts from the opposite strand.

name: antisense promoter activity of L1Hs 5'UTR
description: The promoter activity in the 5' untranslated region (5'UTR) of L1Hs that drives transcription in the antisense direction.

name: antisense transcription
description: Antisense transcription refers to the production of RNA from the non-coding strand of a DNA sequence, which may regulate gene expression.

name: antiviral signaling
description: Antiviral signaling is the innate immune pathway activated by detection of viral or viral-like nucleic acids, such as dsRNA.

name: apoptosis
description: 细胞凋亡，一种程序性细胞死亡过程。

name: apoptosis induction
description: Apoptosis induction refers to the triggering of programmed cell death.

name: arachidonic acid metabolic pathway activation
description: Arachidonic acid (AA) metabolic pathway activation involves the upregulation of metabolic processes related to arachidonic acid.

name: arms race
description: The evolutionary conflict between transposons and host defense mechanisms, such as KRAB-zinc finger genes.

name: association with the nuclear lamina
description: The physical anchoring or localization of genomic regions, such as L1 elements, to the nuclear periphery, a heterochromatic compartment.

name: asymmetric segregation of H3K9me3 at replication forks
description: Preferential transfer of parental H3K9me3 onto the leading strands of replication forks during DNA replication.

name: autonomous L1 transcription
description: Autonomous L1 transcription is the process by which the L1 element's internal promoter drives its own expression, which is critical for initiating retrotransposition.

name: autonomous LINE-1 activity
description: The process by which LINE-1 elements actively retrotranspose within the genome.

name: autonomous replication
description: Autonomous replication is the ability of LINE-1 elements to copy and move themselves within the genome.

name: autonomous retrotransposition
description: Autonomous retrotransposition is the process by which transposons, such as LINE-1, encode the proteins necessary for their own mobilization within the genome.

name: autonomous transposition
description: A small number of LINE-1 copies remain capable of autonomous transposition.

name: bHLH/PAS转录因子依赖的逆转录转座
description: 一种由bHLH/PAS家族转录因子介导的LINE-1逆转录转座模式。

name: basic biological processes
description: Basic biological processes are fundamental cellular and molecular activities essential for life.

name: biomarker detection
description: Biomarker detection is the identification of biological molecules that indicate a specific physiological or pathological state.

name: biomarkers identification
description: The process of discovering measurable indicators of a biological state or condition, often for disease diagnosis or prognosis.

name: bivalent histone mark regulation
description: The process involving both activating and repressive histone modifications to control gene or element activity.

name: brain-specific insertion
description: 脑特异性插入，指转座子插入事件特异性地发生在脑组织中。

name: breakage-fusion-bridge cycle
description: Breakage-fusion-bridge cycle is a mechanism of genomic instability where a broken chromosome end fuses to another, leading to bridge formation and breakage during cell division, causing amplifications or deletions.

name: broad TE upregulation
description: Broad TE upregulation refers to a widespread increase in the transcription levels of various transposable elements.

name: broad-spectrum suppression
description: Broad-spectrum suppression refers to a mechanism that inhibits a wide range of targets, such as multiple TE families.

name: burden
description: 负荷/负担，指个体携带特定遗传元件（如高活性RC-L1）的数量或组合。

name: cAMP-responsive promoter activity
description: The ability of the core promoter region within the ERVWE1 5' LTR to initiate transcription in response to cyclic AMP signaling.

name: cAMP反应元件结合蛋白激活
description: 一种转录因子的激活过程，在FICZ诱导的LINE-1逆转录转座中发挥作用。

name: cDNA synthesis
description: cDNA合成，指LINE-1 ORF2p在逆转录过程中启动并进行的互补DNA链合成。

name: cDNA synthesis initiation
description: cDNA synthesis initiation is the beginning of complementary DNA synthesis from an RNA template, as performed by the L1 ORF2p enzyme during retrotransposition.

name: cDNA合成
description: 由LINE-1 ORF2蛋白的逆转录酶活性介导的，以RNA为模板合成DNA的过程，是逆转录转座的关键步骤。

name: cGAS-STING pathway
description: A cytosolic DNA sensing pathway that triggers innate immune responses.

name: cGAS-STING pathway activation
description: cGAS-STING通路激活，由LINE-1来源的胞质DNA触发，促进p21依赖性细胞周期阻滞和细胞介导的免疫反应。

name: cGAS-STING通路
description: 由cGAS感知胞质DNA并合成cGAMP，进而激活STING蛋白，最终诱导I型干扰素等免疫反应的信号通路。

name: cGAS-STING通路激活
description: 由细胞质DNA感知触发的信号通路，导致干扰素等促炎因子表达。

name: cGAS/STING-mediated DNA sensing
description: 一种DNA感应通路，在本研究中被证明不是LINE-1触发免疫激活所必需的。

name: calcium homeostasis dysregulation
description: Calcium homeostasis dysregulation refers to the impairment of normal calcium ion balance within cells, which is critical for neuronal function and survival.

name: cancer cell type-specific gene expression
description: Cancer cell type-specific gene expression refers to the unique pattern of gene activity in particular types of cancer cells.

name: cancer genome evolution
description: Cancer genome evolution refers to the dynamic process of genomic changes that occur during tumor development and progression.

name: cancer genome remodeling
description: Cancer genome remodeling refers to the extensive structural and sequence alterations that reshape the genome of cancer cells.

name: cancer progression
description: Cancer progression refers to the advancement and worsening of cancer.

name: canonical NF-κB signaling activation
description: Canonical NF-κB signaling activation is a pathway that leads to the expression of pro-inflammatory and anti-apoptotic genes.

name: carcinogenesis
description: Carcinogenesis is the process by which normal cells are transformed into cancer cells.

name: cell cycle arrest
description: 细胞周期阻滞，逆转录酶抑制剂诱导癌细胞停止分裂的过程。

name: cell cycle exit
description: Cell cycle exit refers to the process where cells cease proliferation and enter a quiescent or differentiated state.

name: cell cycle progression
description: 细胞周期进程，本文中观察到异常的G2/M期积累。

name: cell cycle regulation
description: Cell cycle regulation is the process that controls the progression of a cell through the cell cycle.

name: cell differentiation
description: Cell differentiation is the process by which a less specialized cell becomes a more specialized cell type.

name: cell growth
description: Cell growth refers to the increase in cell number or size.

name: cell growth promotion
description: Cell growth promotion refers to the process of stimulating or accelerating the proliferation of cells.

name: cell invasion
description: Cell invasion is the ability of cells, particularly cancer cells, to migrate and penetrate into surrounding tissues.

name: cell lineage tracking
description: Cell lineage tracking is the use of genetic markers, such as somatic mutations, to trace the developmental history and clonal relationships of cells.

name: cell non-autonomous effects
description: 细胞非自主效应，指一个细胞的表型或命运受到邻近其他细胞影响的现象。

name: cell proliferation
description: Cell proliferation is the process of cell growth and division.

name: cell proliferation and invasion
description: 细胞增殖和侵袭，是肿瘤细胞恶性表型的核心特征。

name: cell targeting
description: The process of directing a molecule or particle to a specific cell type.

name: cell type-specific binding
description: Transcription factor binding events that occur specifically in certain cell types, often associated with a cell type-specific epigenetic landscape.

name: cell-specific gene transfer
description: The process of transferring genetic information specifically between certain types of cells.

name: cellular control of LINE-1 replication
description: Cellular control of LINE-1 replication refers to host mechanisms that suppress LINE-1 retrotransposition.

name: cellular differentiation
description: Cellular differentiation is the process by which a less specialized cell becomes a more specialized cell type.

name: cellular functions
description: Cellular functions refer to the normal biological processes carried out by cells, which can be disrupted by transposon activity.

name: cellular identity alteration
description: A change in the defining characteristics or state of a cell, such as its differentiation status or functional properties.

name: cellular pathways
description: Cellular pathways are series of molecular interactions within a cell that lead to a specific product or change in the cell.

name: cellular proliferation
description: Cellular proliferation refers to the process of cell growth and division.

name: cellular self-renewal
description: 细胞的自我更新能力，受LINE-1活动的影响。

name: centromere structural function
description: Centromere structural function refers to the role of centromeres in chromosome segregation and stability during cell division.

name: cerebral organoid development
description: The growth and maturation of three-dimensional in vitro models that recapitulate aspects of human brain development.

name: cfDNA loss at genomic loci
description: Relative loss of cell-free DNA at specific genomic locations with age.

name: change in gene expression
description: Modulation of the levels at which genes are transcribed, which can be influenced by nearby transposon insertions.

name: change in genome integrity
description: Modifications to the stability and fidelity of the genome, potentially caused by transposon activity.

name: changes in gene expression
description: Changes in gene expression refer to alterations in the level at which a gene is transcribed and translated into a functional product.

name: changes in transcription
description: Changes in transcription refer to significant alterations in gene transcription caused by retroelement insertions.

name: chaperone function
description: Chaperone function involves assisting in the proper folding, assembly, or stabilization of other proteins.

name: characteristic translocation events
description: Specific chromosomal rearrangements that are associated with secondary malignancies, potentially influenced by the nonrandom distribution of retrotransposable elements.

name: chimeric TE-gene transcript formation
description: Chimeric TE-gene transcript formation is the process where transposable element sequences become incorporated into gene transcripts.

name: chimeric gene formation
description: 嵌合基因形成，由LINE-1插入基因序列并影响其转录调控而产生的基因。

name: chromatin accessibility
description: Chromatin accessibility refers to the degree to which nuclear macromolecules can physically access and bind to chromatinized DNA.

name: chromatin accessibility alteration
description: Alteration in chromatin accessibility refers to changes in the compactness of chromatin structure, affecting the ability of transcription factors and other proteins to access DNA.

name: chromatin acetylation
description: Chromatin acetylation is an epigenetic modification that typically relaxes chromatin structure and promotes gene expression.

name: chromatin architecture opening
description: Chromatin architecture opening refers to the transition of chromatin from a condensed to a more accessible state.

name: chromatin architecture regulation
description: Chromatin architecture regulation involves the organization and modification of chromatin structure, which influences gene expression.

name: chromatin compartmentalization
description: The process of organizing the genome into distinct three-dimensional compartments, such as euchromatin and heterochromatin.

name: chromatin contact
description: Chromatin contact refers to the physical proximity between different genomic regions.

name: chromatin decondensation
description: Chromatin decondensation refers to the relaxation of chromatin structure, increasing its accessibility.

name: chromatin environment shaping
description: Chromatin environment shaping refers to the process of influencing the structure and accessibility of chromatin.

name: chromatin folding
description: Chromatin folding is the three-dimensional organization of chromatin within the nucleus.

name: chromatin landscape remodeling
description: Chromatin landscape remodeling refers to the extensive reprogramming and reorganization of chromatin structure.

name: chromatin modifications
description: Chromatin modifications refer to changes in chromatin structure, such as DNA methylation and chromatin accessibility, that regulate gene expression.

name: chromatin opening
description: Chromatin opening refers to the process of making genomic regions more accessible for transcription.

name: chromatin organization
description: Chromatin organization refers to the structural arrangement of DNA and proteins within the nucleus.

name: chromatin organization influence
description: The process by which transposons affect the packaging and arrangement of DNA and histones into chromatin.

name: chromatin regulation through RNA-scaffold complexes
description: Chromatin regulation through RNA-scaffold complexes involves transposon-derived RNAs forming complexes with chromatin regulators to modulate chromatin state.

name: chromatin topology remodeling
description: 染色质拓扑结构重塑，涉及基因组三维结构的改变。

name: chromatin-associated function of LINE-1 RNAs
description: A function where LINE-1 RNAs are involved in organizing chromatin architecture.

name: chromosomal breaks
description: 染色体断裂是指染色体DNA双链发生断裂。

name: chromosomal breaks and rearrangements
description: Chromosomal breaks and rearrangements are structural changes to chromosomes that can result from recombination events between retrotransposons.

name: chromosomal instability
description: Chromosomal instability is a state of increased rate of chromosomal aberrations, including rearrangements and copy number changes.

name: chromosomal integration site selection
description: The process of selecting a specific site in the genome for transposon integration.

name: chromosomal rearrangements
description: 染色体重排是指染色体片段发生位置或方向改变的结构变异。

name: chromosomal structure remodeling
description: Chromosomal structure remodeling refers to the dynamic changes in chromatin architecture and organization.

name: chromosome deletion
description: Chromosome deletion is a type of mutation where a segment of a chromosome is lost.

name: chromosome structure influence
description: The process by which transposons affect the physical organization and architecture of chromosomes.

name: chromosome-wide replication timing control
description: The process that regulates the temporal order of DNA replication across an entire chromosome.

name: chronic inflammation
description: Chronic inflammation is a prolonged and dysregulated immune response that can contribute to tissue damage and disease.

name: circulating DNA methylation analysis
description: The quantitative measurement of DNA methylation levels in cell-free DNA circulating in the blood.

name: cis retrotransposition
description: Cis retrotransposition is the process where LINE-1 retrotransposes a copy of its own RNA.

name: cis-acting transcriptional control
description: A mechanism of transcriptional regulation where a DNA sequence influences the expression of nearby genes.

name: cis-activity
description: cis-activity refers to the autonomous retrotransposition capability of LINE-1 elements.

name: cis-element spread
description: The spread of cis-regulatory elements refers to their distribution across the genome through mechanisms such as transposon mobilization.

name: cis-regulatory activity
description: The ability of a DNA sequence to regulate the expression of nearby genes.

name: cis-regulatory element
description: A cis-regulatory element is a non-coding DNA sequence that regulates the expression of nearby genes.

name: cis-regulatory element activity
description: Cis-regulatory element (CRE) activity refers to the functional state of DNA sequences that regulate the transcription of nearby genes.

name: cis-regulatory element domestication
description: The process by which transposable elements are co-opted into functional cis-regulatory elements, such as enhancers.

name: cis-regulatory element provision
description: Cis-regulatory element provision is the process by which transposons supply regulatory sequences that control nearby gene expression.

name: cis-regulatory element transformation
description: The process by which transposable elements are transformed into cis-regulatory elements such as enhancers or promoters.

name: cis-regulatory function
description: The ability of a genomic element to regulate the expression of nearby or distant genes.

name: cis-regulatory impact
description: The cis-regulatory impact refers to the ability of a genetic element, such as a transposon, to influence the expression of nearby genes.

name: cis-regulatory mechanism
description: A cis-regulatory mechanism involves DNA sequences that regulate the expression of nearby genes.

name: cis-regulatory remodeling
description: Cis-regulatory remodeling involves alterations in the regulatory regions of genes that affect their expression.

name: cis-regulatory role
description: A cis-regulatory role refers to the function of a DNA sequence in regulating the expression of nearby genes.

name: clonal amplification
description: 克隆扩增，指携带特定插入突变的细胞群体在组织中增殖扩大的过程。

name: clonal fixation
description: Clonal fixation refers to a somatic mutation becoming present in all cells of a tumor clone.

name: co-expression of TRPV1 and TRPV3
description: The coordinated expression of the TRPV1 and TRPV3 genes in multiple human tissues.

name: co-expression of transposable elements
description: Co-expression of transposable elements refers to the simultaneous transcription of different transposon families or sub-families within the same cell.

name: co-expression with downstream genes
description: Co-expression with downstream genes refers to the coordinated transcription of a transposable element and neighboring genes.

name: co-option/exaptation
description: The evolutionary process where a genetic sequence, originally with one function (or no function), is recruited for a new, beneficial function in the host organism.

name: coding sequence disruption
description: Coding sequence disruption occurs when a retrotransposon inserts into a gene and interrupts its coding sequence.

name: competition with double-strand break repair
description: 与双链断裂修复竞争是指LINE-1的活性可能干扰或占用细胞修复DNA双链断裂的机制。

name: competitive endogenous RNA (ceRNA) activity
description: Competitive endogenous RNAs (ceRNAs) compete for the binding of the same set of miRNAs targeting protein coding genes, thereby regulating their expression.

name: complex rearrangement
description: Complex rearrangement refers to a genomic alteration involving multiple breakpoints and/or non-homologous end joining, often leading to deletions or duplications.

name: complex translocation
description: Complex translocation involves the exchange of genetic material between two or more chromosomes in a non-reciprocal or intricate manner.

name: computational analysis of NGS data
description: Bioinformatic methods used to process and interpret data from next-generation sequencing experiments.

name: containment of regulatory elements
description: The property of SVA retrotransposons, such as SVA D, to harbor DNA sequences that can regulate reporter gene expression in vitro.

name: contributing to aging
description: L1's contributions to aging processes is an area of active research.

name: contributing to aging diseases
description: L1's contributions to aging diseases is an area of active research.

name: contributing to genetic innovation
description: The role of retrotransposons in introducing new genetic material or regulatory sequences that can drive evolutionary change.

name: control expression of protein-coding genes
description: Control of expression of protein-coding genes refers to the regulation of transcription levels of genes that encode proteins.

name: control of stemness
description: Control of stemness refers to the regulation of properties associated with stem cells, such as self-renewal and pluripotency.

name: coordinated expression with type I IFNs
description: Coordinated expression with type I IFNs indicates that the expression levels of certain genes or elements change in a correlated manner with type I interferons.

name: copy number variation
description: 拷贝数变异，指基因组中特定DNA片段拷贝数增加或减少的结构变异。

name: copy number variation (CNV)
description: Copy number variation is a type of structural variation where the number of copies of a specific DNA segment varies among individuals.

name: cotranscriptional splicing
description: Cotranscriptional splicing refers to significant alterations in splicing caused by retroelement insertions.

name: create chimeric transcripts with host genes
description: Creation of chimeric transcripts with host genes refers to the formation of fusion RNAs that include sequences from both a transposable element and a host gene.

name: creating genetic variation
description: Retrotransposons are responsible for creating genetic variation within the human genome.

name: creation of new exon
description: Creation of a new exon refers to the process by which a DNA sequence is incorporated into a gene's transcript as a coding or non-coding segment.

name: cryptic promoter activation
description: Cryptic promoter activation is the process by which a normally silent or inactive promoter region becomes transcriptionally active, often due to a genomic alteration.

name: ctDNA integration
description: The process by which circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) is inserted into the genome of a recipient cell.

name: cut-and-paste mechanism
description: A transposon-based mechanism involving the excision and reinsertion of genetic material.

name: cut-and-paste transposition
description: A mechanism of transposition where the transposon is excised from its original location and inserted into a new one.

name: cytoplasmic RNA granule formation
description: 细胞质RNA颗粒形成，指RNA结合蛋白（如ORF1p）与RNA在细胞质中组装成颗粒状结构。

name: cytoplasmic cDNA synthesis
description: 细胞质cDNA合成，指在细胞质中独立于基因组整合位点发生的逆转录过程。

name: cytoplasmic synthesis of Alu complementary DNA
description: The process of reverse transcribing Alu RNA into complementary DNA (cDNA) in the cytoplasm, independently of genomic integration.

name: cytosolic priming
description: Cytosolic priming refers to the initiation of reverse transcription in the cell cytoplasm, potentially by short RNAs or hairpins.

name: dGTP-triggered tetramer formation
description: dGTP-triggered tetramer formation is a structural change in the SAMHD1 protein required for its dNTPase activity and LINE-1 regulation.

name: dGTP依赖的SAMHD1四聚体形成
description: SAMHD1蛋白在dGTP结合诱导下形成同源四聚体的过程。

name: dNTPase activity
description: dNTPase activity is the enzymatic function of hydrolyzing deoxynucleoside triphosphates.

name: dNTPase活性
description: SAMHD1蛋白水解脱氧核苷三磷酸（dNTP）的酶活性。

name: de novo L1 insertion
description: 新发LINE-1插入，指在个体体细胞或肿瘤细胞中新产生的LINE-1插入事件。

name: de novo L1 insertions
description: 新的LINE-1插入，指在神经元前体细胞中发生的LINE-1逆转录转座事件，导致基因组中产生新的插入位点。

name: de novo L1 integration
description: De novo L1 integration is the process by which a new LINE-1 copy inserts into the genome, initiated by the endonuclease domain nicking a specific DNA sequence.

name: de novo LINE-1 insertion
description: LINE-1的新插入事件，可能驱动肿瘤发生。

name: de novo genetic variation
description: De novo genetic variation refers to new genetic changes that arise in an individual's germline or somatic cells and are not inherited from parents.

name: de novo genomic insertions
description: De novo genomic insertions are new insertions of transposable elements into the genome, which can be detected as evidence of retrotransposition activity.

name: de novo somatic retrotransposition
description: 新发的体细胞逆转录转座事件，指在个体发育过程中新发生的转座子插入。

name: de novo突变
description: 新发生的基因突变，包括由转座子插入引起的缺失、重复和重排。

name: deamination-dependent inhibition
description: Deamination-dependent inhibition is a mechanism of inhibiting LINE-1 retrotransposition that relies on the cytosine deaminase activity of APOBEC3 proteins, leading to cDNA degradation.

name: deamination-independent restriction
description: Deamination-independent restriction is a mechanism of inhibiting LINE-1 retrotransposition that does not rely on the cytosine deaminase activity of APOBEC3 proteins.

name: decreased copy number
description: 拷贝数减少，指基因组中特定DNA序列（如转座子）的数量减少。

name: decreased degradation of L1 DNA or RNA
description: Decreased degradation of L1 DNA or RNA refers to the reduced breakdown of LINE-1 nucleic acids.

name: defending virus infection
description: Defending virus infection is a function where retrotransposons may play a role in the cellular antiviral response.

name: defining chromatin TAD and loop boundaries
description: Defining chromatin topologically associating domain (TAD) and loop boundaries refers to the role of sequences in organizing the 3D structure of chromatin.

name: degradation of LINE-1 RNA
description: Degradation of LINE-1 RNA is a host control mechanism that reduces the availability of the RNA template required for LINE-1 retrotransposition.

name: deleterious L1 insertions
description: 有害的LINE-1插入，指可能导致基因功能丧失或改变的LINE-1插入事件。

name: deletion of target genomic DNA
description: The loss of a segment of DNA at the site of an L1 insertion.

name: deletions
description: 缺失是指基因组中DNA片段的丢失。

name: demethylation
description: 去甲基化，指DNA上CpG位点甲基化修饰的丢失，在癌症中与年轻LINE-1的活性相关。

name: demethylation of repetitive elements
description: Demethylation of repetitive elements, including LINE-1, is a process contributing to genome-wide hypomethylation in cancers.

name: deposition of repressive histone marks
description: Deposition of repressive histone marks is an epigenetic mechanism that leads to gene silencing.

name: deposition of repressive histone modifications
description: The addition of chemical marks to histone proteins that lead to a compact, transcriptionally inactive chromatin state.

name: deregulation of let-7 miRNA expression
description: Deregulation of let-7 miRNA expression refers to abnormal levels or activity of the let-7 microRNA family.

name: derepression in cancer
description: Derepression in cancer refers to the loss of normal silencing mechanisms, leading to the reactivation or increased expression of genes or transposons in cancer cells.

name: developmental plasticity
description: Developmental plasticity is the ability of cells to change their phenotype during development.

name: developmental reversion
description: Developmental reversion refers to the process where cells revert to an earlier developmental state, such as naive human embryonic stem cells reverting to 8-cell-like cells.

name: developmentally programmed genetic switches
description: Permanent and heritable changes in DNA that control precise gene expression during embryonic development.

name: differential DNA methylation
description: Differential DNA methylation refers to changes in the methylation status of specific genomic loci, which can alter gene expression.

name: differential expression of proximal genes
description: Differential expression of proximal genes refers to changes in the expression levels of genes located near a genomic locus, such as a retrotransposon.

name: differential transposon insertion
description: The presence or absence of a transposon insertion at a specific genomic locus when comparing two genomes.

name: differentiation potential
description: 细胞分化为特定类型细胞的能力，受LINE-1活动的影响。

name: disease-causing mutation
description: Retrotransposons are responsible for creating disease-causing mutations within the human genome.

name: disrupt gene function
description: Disruption of gene function refers to the impairment of a gene's normal activity, often through mutation.

name: disruption
description: The interruption or alteration of a genomic sequence or gene function caused by a transposon insertion.

name: disruption of genetic loci at insertion sites
description: 在插入位点破坏基因位点，指逆转录转座的LINE-1序列插入导致基因功能受损。

name: disruption of heterochromatin structure
description: Alteration or destabilization of the condensed, transcriptionally repressive chromatin structure.

name: disruption of mesenchymal to epithelial transition
description: Disruption of mesenchymal to epithelial transition refers to the interference with the cellular process where mesenchymal cells transform into epithelial cells, which is crucial for organogenesis.

name: disruption of nuclear integrity
description: Impairment of the structural and functional integrity of the cell nucleus, including the nuclear envelope.

name: disruption of nucleocytoplasmic transport
description: Impairment of the transport of molecules between the nucleus and the cytoplasm.

name: distal enhancer function
description: A role in regulating gene expression from a distance, typically by looping to interact with gene promoters.

name: distal enhancer regulation
description: The process of controlling gene expression from regulatory elements located far from the gene promoter.

name: disturbance of normal gene expression
description: Disturbance of normal gene expression refers to the abnormal activation or repression of gene transcription, potentially contributing to disease.

name: double-strand break
description: A double-strand break is a type of DNA damage where both strands of the DNA double helix are severed.

name: double-strand break repair
description: 双链断裂修复，是细胞修复DNA双链断裂的机制。

name: double-strand break repair (DSBR)
description: Double-strand break repair is a cellular mechanism to fix DNA double-strand breaks.

name: double-strand breaks
description: Double-strand breaks are a type of DNA damage where both strands of the DNA double helix are severed.

name: double-strand breaks (DSBs)
description: Double-strand breaks are a type of DNA damage where both strands of the DNA double helix are severed.

name: double-stranded RNA formation
description: Double-stranded RNA formation refers to the process where complementary RNA strands base-pair to form dsRNA structures.

name: downregulation
description: 表达下调，指基因或转座子表达水平异常降低。

name: downregulation of AMPKalpha
description: AMPKα水平下调，AMPK是细胞能量稳态和应激反应的关键调节因子。

name: downregulation of mTORC1 activity
description: mTORC1活性下调，mTORC1是调控细胞生长、增殖和存活的关键信号复合物。

name: driving mutation
description: Driving mutation refers to genetic alterations that contribute to the initiation or progression of a disease, such as tumorigenesis.

name: dsRNA formation
description: Transposable elements can fold into double-stranded RNAs (dsRNAs).

name: dsRNA-mediated regulation of L1-5'UTR promoter activity
description: A regulatory mechanism for L1-5'UTR promoter activity involving double-strand RNA (dsRNA).

name: duplication event
description: A duplication event is the process by which a segment of DNA is copied and inserted elsewhere in the genome.

name: dysfunctional metabolism
description: 代谢功能障碍，指细胞能量代谢等代谢过程出现异常。

name: dysregulation of cell energy metabolism
description: Dysregulation of cell energy metabolism refers to abnormal control of cellular processes that generate energy, such as glycolysis and oxidative phosphorylation.

name: dysregulation of cell energy metabolism and cytokine/chemotaxin transcription
description: 细胞能量代谢和细胞因子/趋化因子转录失调，是肿瘤发生发展中的重要分子事件。

name: dysregulation of cytokine/chemotaxin transcription
description: Dysregulation of cytokine/chemotaxin transcription refers to abnormal expression of signaling molecules involved in immune cell recruitment and activation.

name: dysregulation of retrotransposon activity
description: 逆转录转座子活性的失调。

name: eIF2 kinase regulation
description: eIF2 kinase regulation involves the control of kinases that phosphorylate eukaryotic initiation factor 2 (eIF2), a key regulator of protein synthesis.

name: eIF2α磷酸化
description: 真核翻译起始因子2α亚基的磷酸化，是细胞应对压力、抑制全局蛋白质翻译的关键信号。

name: eIF3结合
description: 真核起始因子3（eIF3）结合到L1 RNA的5' UTR m6A簇上。

name: eIF4A/eIF4G相互作用减弱
description: 翻译起始复合物关键组分之间相互作用的减少，是翻译抑制的表现之一。

name: early embryonic development
description: Early embryonic development refers to the initial stages of embryo formation following fertilization.

name: early human brain development
description: Early human brain development is the biological process involving the formation and maturation of the brain during embryonic and fetal stages.

name: ectopic recombination
description: Ectopic recombination is a genetic rearrangement caused by recombination between non-allelic sequences, such as those mediated by transposable elements.

name: elevated expression
description: Elevated expression refers to an increase in the transcription or abundance of a gene or element.

name: embryo development
